# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.DatafeedQuoteValues

# Interface: DatafeedQuoteValues

[Charting Library](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library>).DatafeedQuoteValues

This object contains symbol quote values, where a [quote](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/quotes>) represents a set of data describing the current price. The library uses quote data for various trading functionalities, including the [Order Ticket](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/order-ticket>), [Legend](</charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/Legend>), and widgets, such as [Watchlist](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/Watch-List>), [Details](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#details>), [News](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/news>), and [Depth of Market](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#depth-of-market>).

While all properties in this object are marked as optional, populating most of them is required for supporting trading functionalities. Providing values that do not match the expected data types can cause issues such as UI [loading delays](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/Datafeed-Issues#delays-in-trading-platform-ui-elements>) or [missing data](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/Datafeed-Issues#quotes-are-not-displayed-or-refreshed>). See property descriptions for more information.

## Indexable

âª [valueName: `string`]: `string` | `number` | `string`[] | `number`[] | `undefined`

## Properties

### ask

â¢ `Optional` **ask** : `number`

Ask price â the lowest price a seller is willing to accept for a security. The value is displayed in the [Order Ticket](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/order-ticket>) and [Buy/Sell buttons](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#buysell-buttons-and-lines>).

* * *

### bid

â¢ `Optional` **bid** : `number`

Bid price â the highest price a buyer is willing to pay for a security. The value is displayed in the [Order Ticket](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/order-ticket>) and [Buy/Sell buttons](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#buysell-buttons-and-lines>).

* * *

### ch

â¢ `Optional` **ch** : `number`

Price change. It is usually calculated as a difference between the current price and close price of the previous day (regular session). In the UI, `ch` and [chp](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.DatafeedQuoteValues#chp>) are represented as the last day change parameter in _Data Window_. You can also display these values in the [legend](</charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/Legend#last-day-change-values>). If `ch` and `chp` are not provided, `0.00 (0.00%)` is displayed instead.

Note that `ch` and `chp` are **required** for mobile apps. Otherwise, [`NaN` values](</charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/Legend#nan-values-in-legend>) will appear in the legend.

* * *

### chp

â¢ `Optional` **chp** : `number`

Price change percentage. In the UI, `chp` and [ch](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.DatafeedQuoteValues#ch>) are represented as the last day change parameter in _Data Window_. You can also display these values in the [Legend](</charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/Legend#last-day-change-values>). If `ch` and `chp` are not provided, `0.00 (0.00%)` is displayed instead.

Note that `ch` and `chp` are **required** for mobile apps. Otherwise, [`NaN` values](</charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/Legend#nan-values-in-legend>) will appear in the Legend.

* * *

### description

â¢ `Optional` **description** : `string`

A short description of the symbol. This description is displayed in the [Details](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#details>) widget and the tooltip of the [Watchlist](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/Watch-List>) widget.

* * *

### exchange

â¢ `Optional` **exchange** : `string`

The name of the exchange. The exchange name is displayed in the [Details](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#details>) widget.

* * *

### high_price

â¢ `Optional` **high_price** : `number`

Today's high price. The value is displayed in the [Details](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#details>) widget.

* * *

### low_price

â¢ `Optional` **low_price** : `number`

Today's low price. The value is displayed in the [Details](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#details>) widget.

* * *

### lp

â¢ `Optional` **lp** : `number`

The price at which the most recent trade of a security occurred, regardless of whether it was a buy or sell. Required for the [Order Ticket](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/order-ticket>), [Depth of Market](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#depth-of-market>), [Buy/Sell buttons](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#buysell-buttons-and-lines>), and [Details](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#details>) and [Watchlist](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/Watch-List>) widgets.

* * *

### open_price

â¢ `Optional` **open_price** : `number`

Today's opening price

* * *

### original_name

â¢ `Optional` **original_name** : `string`

Original name

* * *

### prev_close_price

â¢ `Optional` **prev_close_price** : `number`

Closing price of the symbol from the previous regular market session.

Required for mobile apps. Otherwise, `NaN` values will appear in the [Legend](</charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/Legend>).

* * *

### rch

â¢ `Optional` **rch** : `number`

Pre-/post-market price change. This value is required to display information on the [extended session](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/Extended-Sessions>) in the [Details](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#details>) widget.

* * *

### rchp

â¢ `Optional` **rchp** : `number`

Pre-/post-market price change percentage. This value is required to display information on the [extended session](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/Extended-Sessions>) the [Details](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#details>) widget.

* * *

### rtc

â¢ `Optional` **rtc** : `number`

Pre-/post-market price. This value is required to display the [pre-/post-market price line](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/Extended-Sessions#enable-the-price-line>) on the chart and information on the extended session in the [Details](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#details>) widget.

* * *

### rtc_time

â¢ `Optional` **rtc_time** : `number`

Pre-/post-market price update time. This value is required to display information on the [extended session](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/Extended-Sessions>) in the [Details](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#details>) widget.

* * *

### short_name

â¢ `Optional` **short_name** : `string`

Short name for a symbol. Short name is used in the title for the [News](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/news>), [Watchlist](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/Watch-List>) and [Details](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#details>) widgets. You can disable the [`prefer_quote_short_name`](</charting-library-docs/latest/customization/Featuresets#prefer_quote_short_name>) to use the [LibrarySymbolInfo.ticker](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#ticker>) value instead.

* * *

### spread

â¢ `Optional` **spread** : `number`

Spread â the difference between the ask and bid prices. The value is displayed between [Buy/Sell buttons](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#buysell-buttons-and-lines>).

* * *

### volume

â¢ `Optional` **volume** : `number`

Today's trading volume. This value is displayed in the [Watchlist](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/Watch-List>) widget.


---

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.DatafeedQuoteValues

# Interface: DatafeedQuoteValues

[Datafeed](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Datafeed>).DatafeedQuoteValues

This object contains symbol quote values, where a [quote](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/quotes>) represents a set of data describing the current price. The library uses quote data for various trading functionalities, including the [Order Ticket](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/order-ticket>), [Legend](</charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/Legend>), and widgets, such as [Watchlist](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/Watch-List>), [Details](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#details>), [News](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/news>), and [Depth of Market](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#depth-of-market>).

While all properties in this object are marked as optional, populating most of them is required for supporting trading functionalities. Providing values that do not match the expected data types can cause issues such as UI [loading delays](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/Datafeed-Issues#delays-in-trading-platform-ui-elements>) or [missing data](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/Datafeed-Issues#quotes-are-not-displayed-or-refreshed>). See property descriptions for more information.

## Indexable

âª [valueName: `string`]: `string` | `number` | `string`[] | `number`[] | `undefined`

## Properties

### ask

â¢ `Optional` **ask** : `number`

Ask price â the lowest price a seller is willing to accept for a security. The value is displayed in the [Order Ticket](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/order-ticket>) and [Buy/Sell buttons](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#buysell-buttons-and-lines>).

* * *

### bid

â¢ `Optional` **bid** : `number`

Bid price â the highest price a buyer is willing to pay for a security. The value is displayed in the [Order Ticket](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/order-ticket>) and [Buy/Sell buttons](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#buysell-buttons-and-lines>).

* * *

### ch

â¢ `Optional` **ch** : `number`

Price change. It is usually calculated as a difference between the current price and close price of the previous day (regular session). In the UI, `ch` and [chp](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.DatafeedQuoteValues#chp>) are represented as the last day change parameter in _Data Window_. You can also display these values in the [legend](</charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/Legend#last-day-change-values>). If `ch` and `chp` are not provided, `0.00 (0.00%)` is displayed instead.

Note that `ch` and `chp` are **required** for mobile apps. Otherwise, [`NaN` values](</charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/Legend#nan-values-in-legend>) will appear in the legend.

* * *

### chp

â¢ `Optional` **chp** : `number`

Price change percentage. In the UI, `chp` and [ch](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.DatafeedQuoteValues#ch>) are represented as the last day change parameter in _Data Window_. You can also display these values in the [Legend](</charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/Legend#last-day-change-values>). If `ch` and `chp` are not provided, `0.00 (0.00%)` is displayed instead.

Note that `ch` and `chp` are **required** for mobile apps. Otherwise, [`NaN` values](</charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/Legend#nan-values-in-legend>) will appear in the Legend.

* * *

### description

â¢ `Optional` **description** : `string`

A short description of the symbol. This description is displayed in the [Details](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#details>) widget and the tooltip of the [Watchlist](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/Watch-List>) widget.

* * *

### exchange

â¢ `Optional` **exchange** : `string`

The name of the exchange. The exchange name is displayed in the [Details](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#details>) widget.

* * *

### high_price

â¢ `Optional` **high_price** : `number`

Today's high price. The value is displayed in the [Details](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#details>) widget.

* * *

### low_price

â¢ `Optional` **low_price** : `number`

Today's low price. The value is displayed in the [Details](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#details>) widget.

* * *

### lp

â¢ `Optional` **lp** : `number`

The price at which the most recent trade of a security occurred, regardless of whether it was a buy or sell. Required for the [Order Ticket](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/order-ticket>), [Depth of Market](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#depth-of-market>), [Buy/Sell buttons](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#buysell-buttons-and-lines>), and [Details](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#details>) and [Watchlist](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/Watch-List>) widgets.

* * *

### open_price

â¢ `Optional` **open_price** : `number`

Today's opening price

* * *

### original_name

â¢ `Optional` **original_name** : `string`

Original name

* * *

### prev_close_price

â¢ `Optional` **prev_close_price** : `number`

Closing price of the symbol from the previous regular market session.

Required for mobile apps. Otherwise, `NaN` values will appear in the [Legend](</charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/Legend>).

* * *

### rch

â¢ `Optional` **rch** : `number`

Pre-/post-market price change. This value is required to display information on the [extended session](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/Extended-Sessions>) in the [Details](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#details>) widget.

* * *

### rchp

â¢ `Optional` **rchp** : `number`

Pre-/post-market price change percentage. This value is required to display information on the [extended session](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/Extended-Sessions>) the [Details](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#details>) widget.

* * *

### rtc

â¢ `Optional` **rtc** : `number`

Pre-/post-market price. This value is required to display the [pre-/post-market price line](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/Extended-Sessions#enable-the-price-line>) on the chart and information on the extended session in the [Details](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#details>) widget.

* * *

### rtc_time

â¢ `Optional` **rtc_time** : `number`

Pre-/post-market price update time. This value is required to display information on the [extended session](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/Extended-Sessions>) in the [Details](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#details>) widget.

* * *

### short_name

â¢ `Optional` **short_name** : `string`

Short name for a symbol. Short name is used in the title for the [News](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/news>), [Watchlist](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/Watch-List>) and [Details](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#details>) widgets. You can disable the [`prefer_quote_short_name`](</charting-library-docs/latest/customization/Featuresets#prefer_quote_short_name>) to use the [LibrarySymbolInfo.ticker](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.LibrarySymbolInfo#ticker>) value instead.

* * *

### spread

â¢ `Optional` **spread** : `number`

Spread â the difference between the ask and bid prices. The value is displayed between [Buy/Sell buttons](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/#buysell-buttons-and-lines>).

* * *

### volume

â¢ `Optional` **volume** : `number`

Today's trading volume. This value is displayed in the [Watchlist](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/Watch-List>) widget.

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.ExitLevel

# Interface: ExitLevel

[Broker](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker>).ExitLevel

Describes a specific [exit level](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/brackets#multiple-exit-levels>) (bracket) for an order or position. Used when [BrokerConfigFlags.supportMultipleExitLevels](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportmultipleexitlevels>) is enabled.

## Hierarchy

  * **`ExitLevel`**

â³ [`ExitLevelTemplate`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.ExitLevelTemplate>)


## Properties

### guaranteedStop

â¢ `Optional` **guaranteedStop** : `number`

Guaranteed stop loss price.

* * *

### id

â¢ **id** : `string`

Unique identifier for the exit level.

* * *

### isExisting

â¢ `Optional` **isExisting** : `boolean`

Indicates whether the exit level already exists.

**`Default`**
    
    
    true  
    

* * *

### qty

â¢ **qty** : `number`

Quantity to close at this level.

* * *

### stopLoss

â¢ `Optional` **stopLoss** : `number`

Stop loss price.

* * *

### takeProfit

â¢ `Optional` **takeProfit** : `number`

Take profit price.

* * *

### trailingStopPips

â¢ `Optional` **trailingStopPips** : `number`

Trailing stop value in pips.


---

Describes a specific [exit level](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/brackets#multiple-exit-levels>) (bracket) for an order or position. Used when [BrokerConfigFlags.supportMultipleExitLevels](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerConfigFlags#supportmultipleexitlevels>) is enabled.

## Hierarchy

  * [`ExitLevel`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ExitLevel>)

â³ **`ExitLevelTemplate`**


## Properties

### guaranteedStop

â¢ `Optional` **guaranteedStop** : `number`

Guaranteed stop loss price.

#### Inherited from

[ExitLevel](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ExitLevel>).[guaranteedStop](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ExitLevel#guaranteedstop>)

* * *

### id

â¢ **id** : `string`

Unique identifier for the exit level.

#### Inherited from

[ExitLevel](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ExitLevel>).[id](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ExitLevel#id>)

* * *

### isExisting

â¢ `Optional` **isExisting** : `boolean`

Indicates whether the exit level already exists.

**`Default`**
    
    
    true  
    

#### Inherited from

[ExitLevel](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ExitLevel>).[isExisting](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ExitLevel#isexisting>)

* * *

### qty

â¢ **qty** : `number`

Quantity to close at this level.

#### Inherited from

[ExitLevel](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ExitLevel>).[qty](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ExitLevel#qty>)

* * *

### slParentPricePercent

â¢ `Optional` **slParentPricePercent** : `number`

* * *

### slPips

â¢ `Optional` **slPips** : `number`

* * *

### slRiskInPercent

â¢ `Optional` **slRiskInPercent** : `number`

* * *

### stopLoss

â¢ `Optional` **stopLoss** : `number`

Stop loss price.

#### Inherited from

[ExitLevel](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ExitLevel>).[stopLoss](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ExitLevel#stoploss>)

* * *

### takeProfit

â¢ `Optional` **takeProfit** : `number`

Take profit price.

#### Inherited from

[ExitLevel](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ExitLevel>).[takeProfit](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ExitLevel#takeprofit>)

* * *

### tpParentPricePercent

â¢ `Optional` **tpParentPricePercent** : `number`

* * *

### tpPips

â¢ `Optional` **tpPips** : `number`

* * *

### tpRiskInPercent

â¢ `Optional` **tpRiskInPercent** : `number`

* * *

### trailingStopPips

â¢ `Optional` **trailingStopPips** : `number`

Trailing stop value in pips.

#### Inherited from

[ExitLevel](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ExitLevel>).[trailingStopPips](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ExitLevel#trailingstoppips>)

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ExportDataOptions

# Interface: ExportDataOptions

[Charting Library](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library>).ExportDataOptions

## Properties

### from

â¢ `Optional` **from** : `number`

Optional timestamp of the first exported bar.

* * *

### includeDisplayedValues

â¢ `Optional` **includeDisplayedValues** : `boolean`

If true then the exported data will include formatted value as displayed to the user.

**`Default`**
    
    
    false  
    

* * *

### includeHiddenStudies

â¢ `Optional` **includeHiddenStudies** : `boolean`

Include hidden studies in the exported data.

**`Default`**
    
    
    false  
    

* * *

### includeOHLCValuesForSingleValuePlots

â¢ `Optional` **includeOHLCValuesForSingleValuePlots** : `boolean`

Include open, high, low, and close values for plots that only display a single value on the chart. For example line series or symbols with visible_plot_set = 'c'.

* * *

### includeOffsetStudyValues

â¢ `Optional` **includeOffsetStudyValues** : `boolean`

Include study data that has a positive offset from the main series data. That is study data that is "to the right of" the last main series data point.

* * *

### includeSeries

â¢ `Optional` **includeSeries** : `boolean`

If true then the exported data will include open, high, low, close values from the main series.

**`Default`**
    
    
    true  
    

* * *

### includeTime

â¢ `Optional` **includeTime** : `boolean`

If true then each exported data item will include a time value.

**`Default`**
    
    
    true  
    

* * *

### includeUserTime

â¢ `Optional` **includeUserTime** : `boolean`

If true then each exported data item will include a user time value. User time is the time that user sees on the chart. This time depends on the selected time zone and resolution.

**`Default`**
    
    
    false  
    

* * *

### includedStudies

â¢ **includedStudies** : readonly `string`[] | `"all"`

If true then each exported data item will include a value for the specified studies.

* * *

### to

â¢ `Optional` **to** : `number`

Optional timestamp of the last exported bar.


---

An array of [FieldDescriptor](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#fielddescriptor>)

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.Favorites

# Interface: Favorites<TChartTypeFavorites>

[Charting Library](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library>).Favorites

Favorites which can be defined within the Widget Constructor options (see [ChartingLibraryWidgetOptions.favorites](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ChartingLibraryWidgetOptions#favorites>)).

## Type parameters

Name| `TChartTypeFavorites`  
---  
  
## Properties

### chartTypes

â¢ `Optional` **chartTypes** : `TChartTypeFavorites`[]

An array of chart types that are marked as favorite. The names of chart types are listed within the [ChartTypeFavorites](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#charttypefavorites>) or [TradingTerminalChartTypeFavorites](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#tradingterminalcharttypefavorites>) type.

Example: `["Area", "Candles"]`.

* * *

### drawingTools

â¢ `Optional` **drawingTools** : [`DrawingToolIdentifier`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#drawingtoolidentifier>)[]

An array of drawing tool identifiers that should be marked as favorite. These will only be applied if there aren't existing favorites.

Example: ['LineToolBrush', 'LineToolCallout', 'LineToolCircle']

* * *

### indicators

â¢ `Optional` **indicators** : `string`[]

An array of indicator titles that are marked as favorite. The names of indicators are identical to the `title` property of the indicator. For built-in indicators this will match the chart UI in the English version.

Example: `["Awesome Oscillator", "Bollinger Bands"]`.

* * *

### intervals

â¢ `Optional` **intervals** : [`ResolutionString`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#resolutionstring>)[]

An array of time intervals that are marked as favorite.

Example: `["D", "2D"]`


---

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.FibcirclesLineToolOverrides

# Interface: FibcirclesLineToolOverrides

[Charting Library](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library>).FibcirclesLineToolOverrides

Override properties for the Fibcircles drawing tool.

## Properties

### linetoolfibcircles.coeffsAsPercents

â¢ **linetoolfibcircles.coeffsAsPercents** : `boolean`

Default value: `false`

* * *

### linetoolfibcircles.fillBackground

â¢ **linetoolfibcircles.fillBackground** : `boolean`

# Interface: FiftyTwoWeekHighLowIndicatorOverrides

[Charting Library](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library>).FiftyTwoWeekHighLowIndicatorOverrides

Overrides for the '52 Week High/Low' indicator.

Use these properties to customize indicator via [IChartWidgetApi.createStudy](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#createstudy>) and [IStudyApi.applyOverrides](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IStudyApi#applyoverrides>).

## Indexable

âª [key: `string`]: [`StudyOverrideValueType`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#studyoverridevaluetype>)

# Interface: HHistPreferences

[Charting Library](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library>).HHistPreferences

Histogram Preferences

## Properties

### colors

â¢ **colors** : `string`[]

Colors for the histogram up and down bars. Example:
    
    
    colors: ['#1592e6', '#fbc123']  
    

* * *

### direction

â¢ **direction** : [`HHistDirection`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.HHistDirection>)

Direction of the histogram. Whether the histogram will be shown on the left or right edge of the chart

* * *

### percentWidth

â¢ **percentWidth** : `number`

Percentage width of the chart for which the histogram should occupy

* * *

### showValues

â¢ **showValues** : `boolean`

Should the values be shown

* * *

### transparencies

â¢ **transparencies** : `number`[]

Transparency values for the histogram

* * *

### valuesColor

â¢ **valuesColor** : `string`

Color for values shown

* * *

### visible

â¢ **visible** : `boolean`

Should the histogram be visible

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.HistoryMetadata

# Interface: HistoryMetadata

[Charting Library](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library>).HistoryMetadata

Information passed to `onHistoryCallback` for getBars.

## Properties

### nextTime

â¢ `Optional` **nextTime** : `number`

The time of the next available bar in history. The time value should be represented with a Unix timestamp in milliseconds.

You can pass the `nextTime` value to the library if there is no data in the time range that the library requests. Therefore, you notify the library about available data before the requested range and ensure that the next data request is for the right range. For more information, refer to the [How nextTime works](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/UDF#how-nexttime-works>) section.

* * *

### noData

â¢ `Optional` **noData** : `boolean`

Optional value indicating to the library that there is no more data on the server.


---

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.HistoryMetadata

# Interface: HistoryMetadata

[Datafeed](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Datafeed>).HistoryMetadata

Information passed to `onHistoryCallback` for getBars.

## Properties

### nextTime

â¢ `Optional` **nextTime** : `number`

The time of the next available bar in history. The time value should be represented with a Unix timestamp in milliseconds.

You can pass the `nextTime` value to the library if there is no data in the time range that the library requests. Therefore, you notify the library about available data before the requested range and ensure that the next data request is for the right range. For more information, refer to the [How nextTime works](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/UDF#how-nexttime-works>) section.

* * *

### noData

â¢ `Optional` **noData** : `boolean`

Optional value indicating to the library that there is no more data on the server.


---

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerAccountInfo

# Interface: IBrokerAccountInfo

[Broker](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker>).IBrokerAccountInfo

## Hierarchy

  * **`IBrokerAccountInfo`**

â³ [`IBrokerTerminal`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerTerminal>)


## Methods

### accountsMetainfo

â¸ **accountsMetainfo**(): `Promise`<[`AccountMetainfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountMetainfo>)[]>

The library calls `accountsMetainfo` to get a list of accounts for a particular user. The method should return an array that contains an ID and name for each account.

Note that if `accountsMetainfo` returns an array containing more than one element, you should implement the [setCurrentAccount](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerAccountInfo#setcurrentaccount>) method. Refer to [Multiple accounts](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/account-manager/multiple-accounts>) for more information.

#### Returns

`Promise`<[`AccountMetainfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountMetainfo>)[]>

* * *

### currentAccount

â¸ **currentAccount**(): [`AccountId`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker#accountid>)

The library calls `currentAccount` to get the current account's ID.

#### Returns

[`AccountId`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker#accountid>)

* * *

### setCurrentAccount

â¸ **setCurrentAccount**(`id`): `void`

The library calls `setCurrentAccount` when users switch accounts using the drop-down menu in the [Account Manager](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/account-manager/>). This method provides your backend server with the ID of the selected account.

Note that `setCurrentAccount` is required if [accountsMetainfo](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerAccountInfo#accountsmetainfo>) returns an array containing more than one element. Refer to [Multiple accounts](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/account-manager/multiple-accounts>) for more information.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `id`| [`AccountId`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker#accountid>)| The ID of the selected account.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`void`


---

## Methods

### accountsMetainfo

â¸ **accountsMetainfo**(): `Promise`<[`AccountMetainfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.AccountMetainfo>)[]>

The library calls `accountsMetainfo` to get a list of accounts for a particular user. The method should return an array that contains an ID and name for each account.

Note that if `accountsMetainfo` returns an array containing more than one element, you should implement the [setCurrentAccount](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerAccountInfo#setcurrentaccount>) method. Refer to [Multiple accounts](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/account-manager/multiple-accounts>) for more information.

The library calls `setCurrentAccount` when users switch accounts using the drop-down menu in the [Account Manager](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/account-manager/>). This method provides your backend server with the ID of the selected account.

Note that `setCurrentAccount` is required if [accountsMetainfo](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerAccountInfo#accountsmetainfo>) returns an array containing more than one element. Refer to [Multiple accounts](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/account-manager/multiple-accounts>) for more information.

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon

# Interface: IBrokerCommon

[Broker](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker>).IBrokerCommon

## Hierarchy

  * **`IBrokerCommon`**

â³ [`IBrokerTerminal`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerTerminal>)


## Methods

### accountManagerInfo

â¸ **accountManagerInfo**(): [`AccountManagerInfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo>)

The library calls `accountManagerInfo` to get information required for building the [Account Manager](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/account-manager/>).

#### Returns

[`AccountManagerInfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo>)

* * *

### chartContextMenuActions

â¸ **chartContextMenuActions**(`context`, `options?`): `Promise`<[`ActionMetaInfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker#actionmetainfo>)[]>

The library calls `chartContextMenuActions` when users open the [context menu](</charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/context-menu>) on the chart. This method also renders the _Trade_ button in the context menu.

This method should return an array of [ActionMetaInfo](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker#actionmetainfo>) elements, each of them representing one context menu item.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `context`| [`TradeContext`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.TradeContext>)| A context object passed by the library.| `options?`| [`DefaultContextMenuActionsParams`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.DefaultContextMenuActionsParams>)| Default options for the context menu action parameters.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`Promise`<[`ActionMetaInfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker#actionmetainfo>)[]>

A promise that resolves to an array of [ActionMetaInfo](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker#actionmetainfo>), which may be empty. In that case, the _Trade_ button will be removed from the context menu.

* * *

### connectionStatus

â¸ **connectionStatus**(): [`ConnectionStatus`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Broker.ConnectionStatus>)

Defines a connection status for the Broker API. If any other value than `1` ("Connected") is returned, the [Account Manager](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/account-manager/>) will display an endless spinner instead of users' trading data. If you want to handle other scenarios, for example when the status is disconnected, you need to manage this scenario within your implementation and use the [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.connectionStatusUpdate](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#connectionstatusupdate>) method.

If the method is not implemented or returns a value other than `1`, the following error will appear in the console: _Method connectionStatus is not properly implemented_.

#### Returns

[`ConnectionStatus`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Broker.ConnectionStatus>)

* * *

### executions

â¸ **executions**(`symbol`): `Promise`<[`Execution`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.Execution>)[]>

The library calls `executions` to request executions for the specified symbol. If you want executions to be displayed on the chart, set the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportExecutions](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportexecutions>) to `true`.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `symbol`| `string`| The symbol identifier.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`Promise`<[`Execution`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.Execution>)[]>

* * *

### formatter

â¸ **formatter**(`symbol`, `alignToMinMove`): `Promise`<[`INumberFormatter`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.INumberFormatter>)>

Provide a custom price formatter for the specified symbol.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `symbol`| `string`| symbol identifier| `alignToMinMove`| `boolean`| align formatted number to the minimum movement amount of the symbol  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`Promise`<[`INumberFormatter`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.INumberFormatter>)>

* * *

### getOrderDialogOptions

â¸ **getOrderDialogOptions**(`symbol`): `Promise`<[`OrderDialogOptions`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.OrderDialogOptions>)>

Implement this method if you want to [add custom fields](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/order-ticket#add-custom-fields>) to the standard Order Ticket.

Use the `symbol` parameter to return customization options for a particular symbol.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `symbol`| `string`| symbol identifier  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`Promise`<[`OrderDialogOptions`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.OrderDialogOptions>)>

* * *

### getPositionDialogOptions

â¸ **getPositionDialogOptions**(`symbol`): `Promise`<[`PositionDialogOptions`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PositionDialogOptions>)>

Implement this method if you want to customize the position dialog.

Use the `symbol` parameter to return customization options for a particular symbol.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `symbol`| `string`| symbol identifier  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`Promise`<[`PositionDialogOptions`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PositionDialogOptions>)>

* * *

### getSymbolSpecificTradingOptions

â¸ **getSymbolSpecificTradingOptions**(`symbol`): `Promise`<[`SymbolSpecificTradingOptions`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions>)>

Implement this method if you want to have custom options available for different symbols.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `symbol`| `string`| symbol identifier  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`Promise`<[`SymbolSpecificTradingOptions`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions>)>

* * *

### individualPositions

â¸ **individualPositions**(): `Promise`<[`IndividualPosition`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IndividualPosition>)[]>

Called by Trading Platform to request [individual positions](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/positions>). Required if the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportPositionNetting](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportpositionnetting>) flag is set to `true`.

#### Returns

`Promise`<[`IndividualPosition`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IndividualPosition>)[]>

* * *

### isTradable

â¸ **isTradable**(`symbol`): `Promise`<`boolean` | [`IsTradableResult`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IsTradableResult>)>

The library calls `isTradable` to check if a symbol can be traded. If the method returns `false`, users will see the _Non-tradable symbol_ message in the UI when creating orders. You can also display a custom message with the reason why the symbol cannot be traded and the possible solution to resolve the issue. To do this, return an `IsTradableResult` object.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `symbol`| `string`| The symbol identifier.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`Promise`<`boolean` | [`IsTradableResult`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IsTradableResult>)>

* * *

### orders

â¸ **orders**(): `Promise`<[`Order`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker#order>)[]>

The library calls `orders` to request data on the user's active orders. This data is displayed on the _Orders and Positions_ pages of the [Account Manager](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/account-manager/#orders-and-positions>).

#### Returns

`Promise`<[`Order`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker#order>)[]>

* * *

### ordersHistory

â¸ **ordersHistory**(): `Promise`<[`Order`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker#order>)[]>

The library calls `ordersHistory` to retrieve past orders for display on the _History_ page of the [Account Manager](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/account-manager/>). This method is required because the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportOrdersHistory](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportordershistory>) flag is enabled by default. If the method is not implemented while the flag is enabled, the Account Manager will not function. For details, refer to the [History](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/account-manager/#history>) section.

Note that returned orders must have a final status: `rejected`, `filled`, or `cancelled`.

#### Returns

`Promise`<[`Order`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker#order>)[]>

* * *

### positions

â¸ **positions**(): `Promise`<[`Position`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.Position>)[]>

Called by Trading Platform to request [positions](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/positions>). Required if the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportPositions](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportpositions>) flag is set to `true`.

#### Returns

`Promise`<[`Position`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.Position>)[]>

* * *

### quantityFormatter

â¸ **quantityFormatter**(`symbol`): `Promise`<[`INumberFormatter`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.INumberFormatter>)>

Provide a custom quantity formatter for the specified symbol.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `symbol`| `string`| symbol identifier  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`Promise`<[`INumberFormatter`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.INumberFormatter>)>

* * *

### spreadFormatter

â¸ **spreadFormatter**(`symbol`): `Promise`<[`INumberFormatter`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.INumberFormatter>)>

Provide a custom spread formatter for the specified symbol.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `symbol`| `string`| symbol identifier  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`Promise`<[`INumberFormatter`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.INumberFormatter>)>

* * *

### symbolInfo

â¸ **symbolInfo**(`symbol`): `Promise`<[`InstrumentInfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.InstrumentInfo>)>

The library calls `symbolInfo` to request symbol information for the [Order Ticket](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/order-ticket>) and [Depth of Market widget](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/depth-of-market>).

Name| Type| Description| `symbol`| `string`| The symbol identifier.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`Promise`<[`InstrumentInfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.InstrumentInfo>)>

  * **`IBrokerCommon`**

## Methods

### accountManagerInfo

â¸ **accountManagerInfo**(): [`AccountManagerInfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.AccountManagerInfo>)

The library calls `accountManagerInfo` to get information required for building the [Account Manager](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/account-manager/>).

#### Returns

[`AccountManagerInfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.AccountManagerInfo>)

* * *

### chartContextMenuActions

â¸ **chartContextMenuActions**(`context`, `options?`): `Promise`<[`ActionMetaInfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#actionmetainfo>)[]>

The library calls `chartContextMenuActions` when users open the [context menu](</charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/context-menu>) on the chart. This method also renders the _Trade_ button in the context menu.

This method should return an array of [ActionMetaInfo](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#actionmetainfo>) elements, each of them representing one context menu item.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `context`| [`TradeContext`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TradeContext>)| A context object passed by the library.| `options?`| [`DefaultContextMenuActionsParams`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.DefaultContextMenuActionsParams>)| Default options for the context menu action parameters.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`Promise`<[`ActionMetaInfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#actionmetainfo>)[]>

A promise that resolves to an array of [ActionMetaInfo](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#actionmetainfo>), which may be empty. In that case, the _Trade_ button will be removed from the context menu.

* * *

### connectionStatus

â¸ **connectionStatus**(): [`ConnectionStatus`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.ConnectionStatus>)

Defines a connection status for the Broker API. If any other value than `1` ("Connected") is returned, the [Account Manager](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/account-manager/>) will display an endless spinner instead of users' trading data. If you want to handle other scenarios, for example when the status is disconnected, you need to manage this scenario within your implementation and use the [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.connectionStatusUpdate](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#connectionstatusupdate>) method.

If the method is not implemented or returns a value other than `1`, the following error will appear in the console: _Method connectionStatus is not properly implemented_.

#### Returns

[`ConnectionStatus`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.ConnectionStatus>)

* * *

### executions

â¸ **executions**(`symbol`): `Promise`<[`Execution`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.Execution>)[]>

The library calls `executions` to request executions for the specified symbol. If you want executions to be displayed on the chart, set the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportExecutions](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerConfigFlags#supportexecutions>) to `true`.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `symbol`| `string`| The symbol identifier.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`Promise`<[`Execution`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.Execution>)[]>

* * *

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `symbol`| `string`| symbol identifier| `alignToMinMove`| `boolean`| align formatted number to the minimum movement amount of the symbol  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`Promise`<[`INumberFormatter`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.INumberFormatter>)>

* * *

Use the `symbol` parameter to return customization options for a particular symbol.

Name| Type| Description| `symbol`| `string`| symbol identifier  
---|---|---

`Promise`<[`OrderDialogOptions`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.OrderDialogOptions>)>

You can also display a custom message with the reason why the symbol cannot be traded and the possible solution to resolve the issue. To do this, return an `IsTradableResult` object.

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerConnectionAdapterFactory

# Interface: IBrokerConnectionAdapterFactory

[Broker](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker>).IBrokerConnectionAdapterFactory

## Methods

### createDelegate

â¸ **createDelegate** <`T`>(): [`IDelegate`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IDelegate>)<`T`>

Creates a Delegate object

#### Type parameters

Name| Type| `T`| extends `Function`  
---|---  
  
#### Returns

[`IDelegate`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IDelegate>)<`T`>

* * *

### createPriceFormatter

â¸ **createPriceFormatter**(`priceScale?`, `minMove?`, `fractional?`, `minMove2?`, `variableMinTick?`): [`IPriceFormatter`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IPriceFormatter>)

Creates a price formatter.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `priceScale?`| `number`| Defines the number of decimal places. It is `10^number-of-decimal-places`. If a price is displayed as `1.01`, `pricescale` is `100`; If it is displayed as `1.005`, `pricescale` is `1000`.| `minMove?`| `number`| The amount of price precision steps for 1 tick. For example, since the tick size for U.S. equities is `0.01`, `minmov` is 1. But the price of the E-mini S&P futures contract moves upward or downward by `0.25` increments, so the `minmov` is `25`.| `fractional?`| `boolean`| For common prices, is `false` or it can be skipped. For more information on fractional prices, refer to [Fractional format](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/Symbology#fractional-format>).| `minMove2?`| `number`| For common prices, is `0` or it can be skipped.| `variableMinTick?`| `string`| For common prices, is `string` (for example, `0.01 10 0.02 25 0.05`) or it can be skipped. For more information, refer to [Variable tick size](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/Symbology#variable-tick-size>).  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

[`IPriceFormatter`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IPriceFormatter>)

* * *

### createWatchedValue

â¸ **createWatchedValue** <`T`>(`value?`): [`IWatchedValue`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IWatchedValue>)<`T`>

Creates a WatchedValue object

#### Type parameters

Name| `T`  
---  
  
#### Parameters

Name| Type| `value?`| `T`  
---|---  
  
#### Returns

[`IWatchedValue`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IWatchedValue>)<`T`>

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost

# Interface: IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost

[Broker](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker>).IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost

The Trading Host is an API for interaction between the Broker API and the library code related to trading. Its main purpose is to receive information from your backend server where trading logic is implemented and provide updates to the library. Refer to the [Core trading concepts](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/>) article for more information.

## Properties

### factory

â¢ **factory** : [`IBrokerConnectionAdapterFactory`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerConnectionAdapterFactory>)

Broker Connection Adapter Factory object

## Methods

### activateBottomWidget

â¸ **activateBottomWidget**(): `Promise`<`void`>

#### Returns

`Promise`<`void`>

**`Deprecated`**

This method will be removed from the library in the next major version. Please use [setAccountManagerVisibilityMode](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#setaccountmanagervisibilitymode>) instead. Activate bottom widget

* * *

### connectionStatusUpdate

â¸ **connectionStatusUpdate**(`status`, `info?`): `void`

Triggers a connection status update throughout the broker by reinvoking [IBrokerCommon.connectionStatus](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon#connectionstatus>).

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `status`| [`ConnectionStatus`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Broker.ConnectionStatus>)| new connection status to propagate internally to the host| `info?`| [`DisconnectionInfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.DisconnectionInfo>)| additional information to provide when entering a disconnected status  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`void`

* * *

### cryptoBalanceUpdate

â¸ **cryptoBalanceUpdate**(`symbol`, `balance`): `void`

Call this method when a broker connection has received a balance update. This method is required by the crypto Order Ticket. It should be implemented when the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportBalances](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportbalances>) flag is set to `true` in [SingleBrokerMetaInfo.configFlags](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.SingleBrokerMetaInfo#configflags>).

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `symbol`| `string`| symbol ID| `balance`| [`CryptoBalance`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.CryptoBalance>)| updated crypto balance  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`void`

* * *

### currentAccountUpdate

â¸ **currentAccountUpdate**(): `void`

Call this method when user account has been changed synchronously. The terminal will re-request all displayed information.

#### Returns

`void`

* * *

### defaultContextMenuActions

â¸ **defaultContextMenuActions**(`context`, `params?`): `Promise`<[`ActionMetaInfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker#actionmetainfo>)[]>

Provides default buy/sell, show properties actions to be returned as a default by [IBrokerCommon.chartContextMenuActions](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon#chartcontextmenuactions>).

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `context`| [`TradeContext`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.TradeContext>)| trade context| `params?`| [`DefaultContextMenuActionsParams`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.DefaultContextMenuActionsParams>)| optional parameters  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`Promise`<[`ActionMetaInfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker#actionmetainfo>)[]>

* * *

### defaultDropdownMenuActions

â¸ **defaultDropdownMenuActions**(`options?`): [`ActionMetaInfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker#actionmetainfo>)[]

Provides default dropdown list of actions. You can use default actions in IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.setButtonDropdownActions

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `options?`| `Partial`<[`DefaultDropdownActionsParams`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.DefaultDropdownActionsParams>)>| options for the dropdown menu actions  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

[`ActionMetaInfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker#actionmetainfo>)[]

* * *

### defaultFormatter

â¸ **defaultFormatter**(`symbol`, `alignToMinMove`): `Promise`<[`INumberFormatter`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.INumberFormatter>)>

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost

# Interface: IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost

[Charting Library](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library>).IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost

The Trading Host is an API for interaction between the Broker API and the library code related to trading. Its main purpose is to receive information from your backend server where trading logic is implemented and provide updates to the library. Refer to the [Core trading concepts](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/>) article for more information.

## Properties

### factory

â¢ **factory** : [`IBrokerConnectionAdapterFactory`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerConnectionAdapterFactory>)

Broker Connection Adapter Factory object

## Methods

### activateBottomWidget

â¸ **activateBottomWidget**(): `Promise`<`void`>

#### Returns

`Promise`<`void`>

**`Deprecated`**

This method will be removed from the library in the next major version. Please use [setAccountManagerVisibilityMode](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#setaccountmanagervisibilitymode>) instead. Activate bottom widget

* * *

### connectionStatusUpdate

â¸ **connectionStatusUpdate**(`status`, `info?`): `void`

Triggers a connection status update throughout the broker by reinvoking [IBrokerCommon.connectionStatus](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerCommon#connectionstatus>).

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `status`| [`ConnectionStatus`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.ConnectionStatus>)| new connection status to propagate internally to the host| `info?`| [`DisconnectionInfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.DisconnectionInfo>)| additional information to provide when entering a disconnected status  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`void`

* * *

### cryptoBalanceUpdate

â¸ **cryptoBalanceUpdate**(`symbol`, `balance`): `void`

Call this method when a broker connection has received a balance update. This method is required by the crypto Order Ticket. It should be implemented when the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportBalances](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerConfigFlags#supportbalances>) flag is set to `true` in [SingleBrokerMetaInfo.configFlags](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SingleBrokerMetaInfo#configflags>).

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `symbol`| `string`| symbol ID| `balance`| [`CryptoBalance`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.CryptoBalance>)| updated crypto balance  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`void`

* * *

Call this method when user account has been changed synchronously. The terminal will re-request all displayed information.

#### Returns

`void`

* * *

â¸ **subscribeSuggestedQtyChange**(`symbol`, `listener`): `void`

Make sure to unsubscribe from these changes using the corresponding [unsubscribeSuggestedQtyChange](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#unsubscribesuggestedqtychange>) method.

#### Returns

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerTerminal

# Interface: IBrokerTerminal

[Broker](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker>).IBrokerTerminal

The Broker API is a key component that enables trading. Its main purpose is to connect TradingView charts with your trading logic. Refer to the [Core trading concepts](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/>) article for more information.

## Hierarchy

  * [`IBrokerCommon`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon>)

  * [`IBrokerAccountInfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerAccountInfo>)

â³ **`IBrokerTerminal`**


## Methods

### accountManagerInfo

â¸ **accountManagerInfo**(): [`AccountManagerInfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo>)

The library calls `accountManagerInfo` to get information required for building the [Account Manager](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/account-manager/>).

#### Returns

[`AccountManagerInfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo>)

#### Inherited from

[IBrokerCommon](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon>).[accountManagerInfo](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon#accountmanagerinfo>)

* * *

### accountsMetainfo

â¸ **accountsMetainfo**(): `Promise`<[`AccountMetainfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountMetainfo>)[]>

The library calls `accountsMetainfo` to get a list of accounts for a particular user. The method should return an array that contains an ID and name for each account.

Note that if `accountsMetainfo` returns an array containing more than one element, you should implement the [setCurrentAccount](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerAccountInfo#setcurrentaccount>) method. Refer to [Multiple accounts](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/account-manager/multiple-accounts>) for more information.

#### Returns

`Promise`<[`AccountMetainfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountMetainfo>)[]>

#### Inherited from

[IBrokerAccountInfo](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerAccountInfo>).[accountsMetainfo](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerAccountInfo#accountsmetainfo>)

* * *

### cancelOrder

â¸ **cancelOrder**(`orderId`): `Promise`<`void`>

The library calls `cancelOrder` to request canceling an order. You should handle this request on your backend side and provide the library with a new order state. To do this, call the [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.orderUpdate](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#orderupdate>) method right afterwards.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `orderId`| `string`| The ID of the order to cancel.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`Promise`<`void`>

* * *

### cancelOrders

â¸ **cancelOrders**(`symbol`, `side`, `ordersIds`): `Promise`<`void`>

The library calls `cancelOrders` to request canceling multiple orders for a symbol. You should handle this request on your backend side and provide the library with a new order states. To do this, call the [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.orderUpdate](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#orderupdate>) method right afterwards.

`cancelOrders` is only called when users click the _CXL all_ button in the [Depth of Market](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/depth-of-market>) widget.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `symbol`| `string`| The symbol identifier.| `side`| [`Side`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Broker.Side>)| An order side.| `ordersIds`| `string`[]| IDs of the orders to cancel. The orders are selected based on the specified `symbol` and `side`.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`Promise`<`void`>

* * *

### chartContextMenuActions

â¸ **chartContextMenuActions**(`context`, `options?`): `Promise`<[`ActionMetaInfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker#actionmetainfo>)[]>

The library calls `chartContextMenuActions` when users open the [context menu](</charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/context-menu>) on the chart. This method also renders the _Trade_ button in the context menu.

This method should return an array of [ActionMetaInfo](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker#actionmetainfo>) elements, each of them representing one context menu item.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `context`| [`TradeContext`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.TradeContext>)| A context object passed by the library.| `options?`| [`DefaultContextMenuActionsParams`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.DefaultContextMenuActionsParams>)| Default options for the context menu action parameters.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`Promise`<[`ActionMetaInfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker#actionmetainfo>)[]>

A promise that resolves to an array of [ActionMetaInfo](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker#actionmetainfo>), which may be empty. In that case, the _Trade_ button will be removed from the context menu.

#### Inherited from

[IBrokerCommon](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon>).[chartContextMenuActions](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon#chartcontextmenuactions>)

* * *

### closeIndividualPosition

â¸ **closeIndividualPosition**(`individualPositionId`, `amount?`, `confirmId?`): `Promise`<`void`>

The library calls `closeIndividualPosition` if the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportCloseIndividualPosition](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportcloseindividualposition>) or [BrokerConfigFlags.supportPartialCloseIndividualPosition](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportpartialcloseindividualposition>) configuration flag is `true`. `closeIndividualPosition` allows [closing an individual position](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/positions#close-positions>) by ID.

Note that the library expects you to call the [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.positionUpdate](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#positionupdate>) method right afterwards. Otherwise, the library will return a [timeout issue](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/common-issues#timeout-issue>).

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `individualPositionId`| `string`| The individual position ID.| `amount?`| `number`| The amount is specified if `supportPartialCloseIndividualPosition` is `true` and the user wants to close only [part of the individual position](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/positions#partial-closing>).| `confirmId?`| `string`| The ID of the confirmed close position preview. This parameter is passed if [`supportPreviewClosePosition`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerConfigFlags#supportpreviewcloseposition>) is `true`.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`Promise`<`void`>

* * *

### closePosition

â¸ **closePosition**(`positionId`, `amount?`, `confirmId?`): `Promise`<`void`>

The library calls `closePosition` to request [closing a position](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/positions#close-positions>) by ID. You should handle this request on your backend side and provide the library with a new position state. To do this, call the [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.positionUpdate](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#positionupdate>) method right afterwards. Otherwise, the library will return a [timeout issue](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/common-issues#timeout-issue>).

`closePosition` is only called if the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportClosePosition](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportcloseposition>) or [BrokerConfigFlags.supportPartialClosePosition](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportpartialcloseposition>) configuration flag is `true`.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `positionId`| `string`| The position ID.| `amount?`| `number`| The amount is specified if [`supportPartialClosePosition`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerConfigFlags#supportpartialcloseposition>) is `true` and the user wants to close only [part of the position](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/positions#partial-closing>).| `confirmId?`| `string`| The ID of the confirmed close position preview. This parameter is passed if [`supportPreviewClosePosition`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerConfigFlags#supportpreviewcloseposition>) is `true`.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`Promise`<`void`>

* * *

### connectionStatus

â¸ **connectionStatus**(): [`ConnectionStatus`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Broker.ConnectionStatus>)

Defines a connection status for the Broker API. If any other value than `1` ("Connected") is returned, the [Account Manager](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/account-manager/>) will display an endless spinner instead of users' trading data. If you want to handle other scenarios, for example when the status is disconnected, you need to manage this scenario within your implementation and use the [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.connectionStatusUpdate](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#connectionstatusupdate>) method.

If the method is not implemented or returns a value other than `1`, the following error will appear in the console: _Method connectionStatus is not properly implemented_.

#### Returns

[`ConnectionStatus`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Broker.ConnectionStatus>)

#### Inherited from

[IBrokerCommon](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon>).[connectionStatus](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon#connectionstatus>)

* * *

### editIndividualPositionBrackets

â¸ **editIndividualPositionBrackets**(`individualPositionId`, `brackets`): `Promise`<`void`>

This method is called if the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportIndividualPositionBrackets](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportindividualpositionbrackets>) configuration flag is on. It displays a dialog that enables take-profit and stop-loss editing.

Note that the library expects you to call the [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.positionUpdate](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#positionupdate>) method right afterwards.

Name| Type| Description| `individualPositionId`| `string`| ID of existing individual position to be modified| `brackets`| [`Brackets`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.Brackets>)| new Brackets to be set for the individual position  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`Promise`<`void`>

* * *

### editPositionBrackets

â¸ **editPositionBrackets**(`positionId`, `brackets`, `customFields?`): `Promise`<`void`>

The library calls `editPositionBrackets` if the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportPositionBrackets](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportpositionbrackets>) configuration flag is `true`. It shows a dialog that enables take-profit and stop-loss editing.

Note that the library expects you to call the [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.positionUpdate](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#positionupdate>) method right afterwards.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `positionId`| `string`| The ID of the existing position to be modified.| `brackets`| [`Brackets`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.Brackets>)| New brackets to be set for the position.| `customFields?`| [`CustomInputFieldsValues`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.CustomInputFieldsValues>)| Custom fields to display in the dialog.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`Promise`<`void`>

### isTradable

â¸ **isTradable**(`symbol`): `Promise`<`boolean` | [`IsTradableResult`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IsTradableResult>)>

The library calls `isTradable` to check if a symbol can be traded. If the method returns `false`, users will see the _Non-tradable symbol_ message in the UI when creating orders. You can also display a custom message with the reason why the symbol cannot be traded and the possible solution to resolve the issue. To do this, return an `IsTradableResult` object.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `symbol`| `string`| The symbol identifier.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`Promise`<`boolean` | [`IsTradableResult`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IsTradableResult>)>

Name| Type| Description| `leverageInfoParams`| [`LeverageInfoParams`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.LeverageInfoParams>)| information about the specific symbol to provide leverage information for

### modifyOrder

â¸ **modifyOrder**(`order`, `confirmId?`): `Promise`<`void`>

The library calls `modifyOrder` to request modifying an existing order. You should handle this request on your backend side and provide the library with a new order state. To do this, call the [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.orderUpdate](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#orderupdate>) method right afterwards. Otherwise, the library will return a [timeout issue](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/common-issues#timeout-issue>).

Note that [market orders](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/orders#order-types>) cannot be modified.

To enable an order preview before modification, set the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportModifyOrderPreview](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportmodifyorderpreview>) configuration flag to `true`. Refer to [Enable order preview](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/order-ticket#enable-order-preview>) for more information.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `order`| [`Order`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker#order>)| Order information.| `confirmId?`| `string`| The ID of the confirmed order. This parameter is passed if [`supportModifyOrderPreview`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerConfigFlags#supportmodifyorderpreview>) is `true`.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`Promise`<`void`>

* * *

### orders

â¸ **orders**(): `Promise`<[`Order`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker#order>)[]>

The library calls `orders` to request data on the user's active orders. This data is displayed on the _Orders and Positions_ pages of the [Account Manager](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/account-manager/#orders-and-positions>).

#### Returns

`Promise`<[`Order`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker#order>)[]>

#### Inherited from

[IBrokerCommon](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon>).[orders](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon#orders>)

* * *

### ordersHistory

â¸ **ordersHistory**(): `Promise`<[`Order`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker#order>)[]>

The library calls `ordersHistory` to retrieve past orders for display on the _History_ page of the [Account Manager](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/account-manager/>). This method is required because the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportOrdersHistory](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportordershistory>) flag is enabled by default. If the method is not implemented while the flag is enabled, the Account Manager will not function. For details, refer to the [History](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/account-manager/#history>) section.

Note that returned orders must have a final status: `rejected`, `filled`, or `cancelled`.

#### Returns

`Promise`<[`Order`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker#order>)[]>

#### Inherited from

[IBrokerCommon](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon>).[ordersHistory](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon#ordershistory>)

* * *

### placeOrder

â¸ **placeOrder**(`order`, `confirmId?`): `Promise`<[`PlaceOrderResult`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PlaceOrderResult>)>

The library calls `placeOrder` to request placing an order pre-filled with partial or complete information. You should handle this request on your backend side. For more information, refer to [Order creation](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#1-order-creation>).

To display an order preview before placing it, set [BrokerConfigFlags.supportPlaceOrderPreview](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportplaceorderpreview>) to `true`. Refer to [Enable order preview](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/order-ticket#enable-order-preview>) for more information.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `order`| [`PreOrder`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PreOrder>)| Order information.| `confirmId?`| `string`| The ID of the confirmed order. This parameter is passed if [`supportPlaceOrderPreview`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerConfigFlags#supportplaceorderpreview>) is `true`.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`Promise`<[`PlaceOrderResult`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PlaceOrderResult>)>

An object with the order ID.

* * *

### previewOrder

â¸ **previewOrder**(`order`): `Promise`<[`OrderPreviewResult`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.OrderPreviewResult>)>

The library calls `previewOrder` to show an order preview when a user clicks _Buy order_ or _Modify order_ in the Order Ticket. To [enable order preview](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/order-ticket#enable-order-preview>), set the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportPlaceOrderPreview](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportplaceorderpreview>) or [BrokerConfigFlags.supportModifyOrderPreview](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportmodifyorderpreview>) configuration flag to `true`.

This method returns estimated commission, fees, margin, and other information for the order without it actually being placed.

Name| Type| Description| `order`| [`PreOrder`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PreOrder>)| Order information.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`Promise`<[`OrderPreviewResult`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.OrderPreviewResult>)>

### setCurrentAccount

â¸ **setCurrentAccount**(`id`): `void`

The library calls `setCurrentAccount` when users switch accounts using the drop-down menu in the [Account Manager](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/account-manager/>). This method provides your backend server with the ID of the selected account.

Note that `setCurrentAccount` is required if [accountsMetainfo](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerAccountInfo#accountsmetainfo>) returns an array containing more than one element. Refer to [Multiple accounts](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/account-manager/multiple-accounts>) for more information.
