# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo

# Interface: LibrarySymbolInfo

[Charting Library](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library>).LibrarySymbolInfo

## Hierarchy

  * **`LibrarySymbolInfo`**

â³ [`SymbolExt`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt>)


## Properties

### base_name

â¢ `Optional` **base_name** : `string`[]

Array of base symbols Example: for `AAPL*MSFT` it is `['NASDAQ:AAPL', 'NASDAQ:MSFT']`

* * *

### build_seconds_from_ticks

â¢ `Optional` **build_seconds_from_ticks** : `boolean`

The boolean value showing whether or not seconds bars for this symbol can be built from ticks. Only available in Trading Platform.

  * [LibrarySymbolInfo.has_seconds](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#has_seconds>) must also be `true`
  * [LibrarySymbolInfo.has_ticks](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#has_ticks>) must also be `true`
  * [LibrarySymbolInfo.seconds_multipliers](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#seconds_multipliers>) must be an empty array or only contain multipliers that the datafeed provides itself


The library builds resolutions from ticks only if there are no seconds resolutions from the datafeed or the provided resolutions are larger then the required one. For example, the datafeed provides `3S` resolution. In this case, the library can build only `1S` or `2S` resolutions from ticks. Resolutions such as `15S` will be build with seconds bars.

**`Default`**
    
    
    false  
    

* * *

### corrections

â¢ `Optional` **corrections** : `string`

List of corrections for a symbol. The corrections are days when the trading session differs from the default one set in [LibrarySymbolInfo.session](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#session>). The `corrections` value is a string in the following format: `SESSION:YYYYMMDD`. For more information, refer to [corrections](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/Symbology#corrections>).

The string below specifies corrections for two trading days:

  * November 13, 2018. This trading day is split into two sessions. The first session starts at 19:00 four days before (November 9, 2018) and ends at 23:50 four days before. The second session starts at 10:00 and ends at 18:45.
  * November 14, 2018. The session starts at 10:00 and ends at 14:00.


**`Example`**
    
    
    "1900F4-2350F4,1000-1845:20181113;1000-1400:20181114"  
    

* * *

### currency_code

â¢ `Optional` **currency_code** : `string`

The currency in which the instrument is traded or some other currency if currency conversion is enabled. It is displayed in the Security Info dialog and on the price axes.

* * *

### daily_multipliers

â¢ `Optional` **daily_multipliers** : `string`[]

An array of daily resolutions that your datafeed supports. Items in the array should be listed in ascending order and **should not** include letters, for example: `["1", "2"]`. This property is required to enable daily resolutions. Refer to the [Configure resolutions in datafeed](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#resolution-in-days>) article for more information.

The library also uses resolutions listed in `daily_multipliers` to display higher resolution that your datafeed does not explicitly support. If `daily_multipliers` is not specified, the library cannot build additional resolutions.

**`Default`**
    
    
    ["1"]  
    

* * *

### data_status

â¢ `Optional` **data_status** : `"streaming"` | `"endofday"` | `"delayed_streaming"`

The status code of a series with this symbol. For `delayed_streaming` and `endofday` type of data, the status is displayed as an icon and the _Data is delayed_ section in the [_Legend_](</charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/Legend#display-delayed-data-information>), next to the market status icon. Note that you should also enable the [`display_data_mode`](</charting-library-docs/latest/customization/Featuresets#display_data_mode>) featureset.

When declaring `delayed_streaming` you also have to specify its [LibrarySymbolInfo.delay](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#delay>) in seconds.

* * *

### delay

â¢ `Optional` **delay** : `number`

Type of delay that is associated to the data or real delay for real time data.

  * `0` for realtime
  * `-1` for endofday
  * or delay in seconds (for delayed realtime)


* * *

### description

â¢ **description** : `string`

The description of the symbol. Will be displayed in the chart legend for this symbol.

* * *

### exchange

â¢ **exchange** : `string`

Traded exchange (current (proxy) exchange). The name will be displayed in the chart legend for this symbol.

**`Example`**
    
    
    "NYSE"  
    

* * *

### exchange_logo

â¢ `Optional` **exchange_logo** : `string`

URL of image to be displayed as the logo for the exchange. The `show_exchange_logos` featureset needs to be enabled for this to be visible in the UI.

The image should ideally be square in dimension. You can use any image type which the browser supports natively. Simple SVG images are recommended.

Examples:

  * `https://yourserver.com/exchangeLogo.svg`
  * `/images/myImage.png`
  * `data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3...`
  * `data:image/jpeg;base64,/9j/4AAQSkZJRgABAQAASABIAAD/4...`


* * *

### expiration_date

â¢ `Optional` **expiration_date** : `number`

Unix timestamp of the expiration date. One must set this value when `expired` = `true`. The library will request data for this symbol starting from that time point.

* * *

### expired

â¢ `Optional` **expired** : `boolean`

Boolean showing whether this symbol is expired futures contract or not.

**`Default`**
    
    
    false  
    

* * *

### format

â¢ **format** : [`SeriesFormat`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#seriesformat>)

Format of displaying labels on the price scale:

`price` \- formats decimal or fractional numbers based on `minmov`, `pricescale`, `minmove2`, `fractional` and `variableMinTick` values. See [Price format](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/Symbology#price-format>) for more details. `volume` \- formats decimal numbers in thousands, millions, billions or trillions

* * *

### fractional

â¢ `Optional` **fractional** : `boolean`

For common prices this can be skipped.

Fractional prices are displayed 2 different forms: 1) `xx'yy` (for example, `133'21`) 2) `xx'yy'zz` (for example, `133'21'5`).

  * `xx` is an integer part.
  * `minmov/pricescale` is a Fraction.
  * `minmove2` is used in form 2.
  * `fractional` is `true`.
  * `variableMinTick` is skipped.


Example:

If `minmov = 1`, `pricescale = 128` and `minmove2 = 4`:

  * `119'16'0` represents `119 + 16/32`
  * `119'16'2` represents `119 + 16.25/32`
  * `119'16'5` represents `119 + 16.5/32`
  * `119'16'7` represents `119 + 16.75/32`


More examples:

  * `ZBM2014 (T-Bond)` with `1/32`: `minmov = 1`, `pricescale = 32`, `minmove2 = 0`
  * `ZCM2014 (Corn)` with `2/8`: `minmov = 2`, `pricescale = 8`, `minmove2 = 0`
  * `ZFM2014 (5 year t-note)` with `1/4 of 1/32`: `minmov = 1`, `pricescale = 128`, `minmove2 = 4`


* * *

### has_daily

â¢ `Optional` **has_daily** : `boolean`

A flag indicating whether your datafeed contains daily data for this symbol. If `true`, the library requests this data when a daily resolution is selected. If `false`, _No data here_ is displayed on the chart.

`has_daily` is set to `true` by default. However, you should also specify [daily_multipliers](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#daily_multipliers>) to enable daily resolutions. Refer to the [Configure resolutions in datafeed](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#resolution-in-days>) article for more information.

**`Default`**
    
    
    true  
    

* * *

### has_empty_bars

â¢ `Optional` **has_empty_bars** : `boolean`

The boolean value showing whether the library should generate empty bars in the session when there is no data from the data feed for this particular time.

I.e., if your session is `0900-1600` and your data has gaps between `11:00` and `12:00` and your `has_empty_bars` is `true`, then the Library will fill the gaps with bars for this time.

Flag `has_empty_bars` = `true` cannot be used if featureset `disable_resolution_rebuild` is enabled.

**`Default`**
    
    
    false  
    

* * *

### has_intraday

â¢ `Optional` **has_intraday** : `boolean`

A flag indicating whether your datafeed contains intraday (minutes) data for this symbol. If `true`, the library requests this data when an intraday resolution is selected. If `false`, _No data here_ is displayed on the chart.

This property is required to enable intraday resolutions. Refer to the [Configure resolutions in datafeed](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#resolution-in-minutes-intraday>) article for more information.

**`Default`**
    
    
    false  
    

* * *

### has_seconds

â¢ `Optional` **has_seconds** : `boolean`

A flag indicating whether your datafeed contains seconds data for this symbol. If `true`, the library requests this data when a seconds resolution is selected. If `false`, _No data here_ is displayed on the chart.

You should set `has_seconds` to `true` to enable seconds resolutions. Refer to the [Configure resolutions in datafeed](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#resolution-in-seconds>) article for more information.

**`Default`**
    
    
    false  
    

* * *

### has_ticks

â¢ `Optional` **has_ticks** : `boolean`

A flag indicating whether your datafeed contains ticks data for this symbol. If `true`, the library requests this data when a resolution in ticks is selected. If `false`, _No data here_ is displayed on the chart.

You should set `has_ticks` to `true` to enable ticks resolutions. Refer to the [Configure resolutions in datafeed](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#resolution-in-ticks>) article for more information.

**`Default`**
    
    
    false  
    

* * *

### has_weekly_and_monthly

â¢ `Optional` **has_weekly_and_monthly** : `boolean`

A flag indicating whether your datafeed contains weekly or monthly data for this symbol. If `true`, the library requests this data when the corresponding resolution is selected. To enable weekly or monthly resolutions, you should also specify the [weekly_multipliers](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#weekly_multipliers>) or [monthly_multipliers](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#monthly_multipliers>) properties. Refer to the [Configure resolutions in datafeed](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#resolution-in-weeks--months>) article for more information.

If `has_weekly_and_monthly` is set to `false`, the library attempts to build the resolutions using daily bars. Note that building bars requires a large number of requests to your datafeed. If the library fails to build bars, _No data here_ is displayed on the chart.

**`Default`**
    
    
    false  
    

* * *

### industry

â¢ `Optional` **industry** : `string`

Industry for stocks to be displayed in the Security Info.

* * *

### intraday_multipliers

â¢ `Optional` **intraday_multipliers** : `string`[]

An array of intraday (minutes) resolutions that your datafeed supports. Items in the array should be listed in ascending order, for example: `["1", "2"]`.

This property is required to enable intraday resolutions. Refer to the [Configure resolutions in datafeed](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#resolution-in-minutes-intraday>) article for more information. Note that each resolution in `intraday_multipliers` should be handled in the [IDatafeedChartApi.getBars](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi#getbars>) implementation. Consider the [example](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#example>).

The library also uses resolutions listed in `intraday_multipliers` to display higher resolution that your datafeed does not explicitly support. If `intraday_multipliers` is not specified, the library cannot build additional resolutions.

Note that the library **cannot** build daily, weekly, or monthly resolutions using intraday data.

**`Default`**
    
    
    [] â specifies that the datafeed can provide data for any requested resolution.  
    

* * *

### library_custom_fields

â¢ `Optional` **library_custom_fields** : `Record`<`string`, `unknown`>

Additional metadata to include with the symbol information. These parameters will not affect existing properties such as 'ticker' or 'name' and will not be processed by the library. However, they can be accessed later through the [IChartWidgetApi.symbolExt](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#symbolext>) method, impacting existing properties such as 'ticker' or 'name'.

* * *

### listed_exchange

â¢ **listed_exchange** : `string`

short name of the exchange where this symbol is traded (real listed exchange). The name will be displayed in the chart legend for this symbol.

**`Example`**
    
    
    "NYSE"  
    

* * *

### logo_urls

â¢ `Optional` **logo_urls** : [`string`, `string`] | [`string`]

URL of image/s to be displayed as the logo/s for the symbol. The `show_symbol_logos` featureset needs to be enabled for this to be visible in the UI.

  * If a single url is returned then that url will solely be used to display the symbol logo.
  * If two urls are provided then the images will be displayed as two partially overlapping circles with the first url appearing on top. This is typically used for FOREX where you would like to display two country flags are the symbol logo.


The image/s should ideally be square in dimension. You can use any image type which the browser supports natively.

Examples:

  * `https://yourserver.com/apple.svg`
  * `/images/myImage.png`
  * `data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3...`
  * `data:image/jpeg;base64,/9j/4AAQSkZJRgABAQAASABIAAD/4...`


* * *

### long_description

â¢ `Optional` **long_description** : `string`

Symbol Long description

Optional long(er) description for the symbol.

* * *

### minmov

â¢ **minmov** : `number`

The number of units that make up one tick. For example, U.S. equities are quotes in decimals, and tick in decimals, and can go up +/- `.01`. Therefore, the tick increment is 1 and `minmov = 1`. But the e-mini S&P futures contract, though quoted in decimals, goes up in `.25` increments, so the tick increment is 25 and `minmov = 25`. Refer to [Price format](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/Symbology#price-format>) for more information.

* * *

### minmove2

â¢ `Optional` **minmove2** : `number`

This property is used to display prices in the [fraction of a fraction](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/Symbology#fraction-of-a-fraction-format>) format. For example, the ZBM2023 tick size is 1/4 of a 32nd. To display this security, set `minmov = 1`, `pricescale = 128`, `minmove2 = 4`.

For common prices, `minmove2` can be skipped or set to `0`.

* * *

### monthly_multipliers

â¢ `Optional` **monthly_multipliers** : `string`[]

An array of monthly resolutions that your datafeed supports. Items in the array should be listed in ascending order and **should not** include letters, for example: `["1", "3", "6", "12"]`. This property is required to enable monthly resolutions. Refer to the [Configure resolutions in datafeed](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#resolution-in-weeks--months>) article for more information.

The library also uses resolutions listed in `monthly_multipliers` to display higher resolution that your datafeed does not explicitly support. If `monthly_multipliers` is not specified, the library cannot build additional resolutions.

**`Default`**
    
    
    ['1']  
    

* * *

### name

â¢ **name** : `string`

It is a symbol name within an exchange, such as `AAPL` or `9988` (Hong Kong). Note that it should not contain the exchange name. This symbol name is visible to users and can be repeated.

By default, `name` is used to resolve symbols in the [Datafeed API](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/datafeed-api/>). If you use [LibrarySymbolInfo.ticker](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#ticker>), the library will use the ticker for Datafeed API requests.

* * *

### original_currency_code

â¢ `Optional` **original_currency_code** : `string`

The currency in which the instrument is traded.

* * *

### original_unit_id

â¢ `Optional` **original_unit_id** : `string`

A unique identifier of a unit in which the instrument is traded.

* * *

### price_source_id

â¢ `Optional` **price_source_id** : `string`

Optional ID of a price source for a symbol. Should match one of the price sources from the [price_sources](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#price_sources>) array.

Note that you should set the [`symbol_info_price_source`](</charting-library-docs/latest/customization/Featuresets#symbol_info_price_source>) featureset to `true` to display the symbol price source in the main series legend.

* * *

### price_sources

â¢ `Optional` **price_sources** : [`SymbolInfoPriceSource`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolInfoPriceSource>)[]

Supported price sources for the symbol. Price sources appear in the series legend and indicate the origin of values represented by symbol bars. Example price sources: "Spot Price", "Ask", "Bid", etc. The price source information is valuable when viewing non-OHLC series types.

Note that you should set the [`symbol_info_price_source`](</charting-library-docs/latest/customization/Featuresets#symbol_info_price_source>) featureset to `true` to display the symbol price source in the main series legend.

**`Example`**
    
    
    [{ id: '1', name: 'Spot Price' }, { id: '321', name: 'Bid' }]  
    

* * *

### pricescale

â¢ **pricescale** : `number`

A number of decimal places or fractions that the price has.

The `pricescale` value depends on the [price format](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/Symbology#price-format>). If you want to display the price in the decimal format, `pricescale` should be `10^n`, where `n` is the number of decimal places. **Examples:** `1`, `10`, `10000000`.

If you want to display the price in the fractional format, `pricescale` should be `2^n`. This value represents the number of fractions. **Examples:** `8`, `16`, `256`.

* * *

### seconds_multipliers

â¢ `Optional` **seconds_multipliers** : `string`[]

An array of seconds resolutions that your datafeed supports. Items in the array should be listed in ascending order and **should not** include letters, for example: `["1", "2"]`. This property is required to enable seconds resolutions. Refer to the [Configure resolutions in datafeed](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#resolution-in-seconds>) article for more information.

The library also uses resolutions listed in `seconds_multipliers` to display higher resolution that your datafeed does not explicitly support. If `seconds_multipliers` is not specified, the library cannot build additional resolutions. Consider the example. You need to enable one-second and five-second resolutions but your datafeed contains only one-second data. In this case, you should set `seconds_multipliers` to `["1"]`. The library will build the five-second resolution from one-second data.

* * *

### sector

â¢ `Optional` **sector** : `string`

Sector for stocks to be displayed in the Security Info.

* * *

### session

â¢ **session** : `string`

Trading hours for the symbol. The time should be in the **exchange** time zone specified in the [LibrarySymbolInfo.timezone](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#timezone>) property. Refer to the [Trading sessions](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/Trading-Sessions>) article for more information.

**`Example`**
    
    
    "1700-0200"  
    

* * *

### session_display

â¢ `Optional` **session_display** : `string`

The session value to display in the UI. If not specified, then `session` is used.

* * *

### session_holidays

â¢ `Optional` **session_holidays** : `string`

A string that contains a list of non-trading holidays for the symbol. Holiday dates should be in the `YYYYMMDD` format. These dates are not displayed on the chart.

You can specify a correction for a holiday using [LibrarySymbolInfo.corrections](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#corrections>).

**`Example`**
    
    
    "20181105,20181107,20181112"  
    

* * *

### subsession_id

â¢ `Optional` **subsession_id** : `string`

An ID of a subsession specified in [subsessions](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#subsessions>). The value must match the subsession that is currently displayed on the chart. For more information, refer to the [Extended sessions](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/Symbology#extended-sessions>) section.

* * *

### subsessions

â¢ `Optional` **subsessions** : [`LibrarySubsessionInfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySubsessionInfo>)[]

An array of objects that contain information about certain subsessions within the extended session. For more information, refer to the [Extended sessions](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/Symbology#extended-sessions>) section.

* * *

### supported_resolutions

â¢ `Optional` **supported_resolutions** : [`ResolutionString`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#resolutionstring>)[]

An array of [resolutions](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions>) which should be enabled in the _Resolution_ drop-down menu for this symbol. Each item of the array is expected to be a string that has a specific [format](</charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/Resolution#resolution-format>).

If one changes the symbol and the new symbol does not support the selected resolution, an error message will be shown on the chart.

**Resolution availability logic (pseudocode):**
    
    
    resolutionAvailable  =  
        resolution.isIntraday  
            ? symbol.has_intraday && symbol.supported_resolutions(resolution)  
            : symbol.supported_resolutions(resolution);  
    

If `supported_resolutions` is `[]` (empty array), all resolutions are disabled in the _Resolution_ drop-down menu.

If `supported_resolutions` is `undefined`, all resolutions that the chart support ([DatafeedConfiguration.supported_resolutions](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.DatafeedConfiguration#supported_resolutions>)) and custom resolutions are enabled.

Note that the list of available time frames depends on supported resolutions. Time frames that require resolutions that are unavailable for a particular symbol will be hidden. Refer to [Time frame toolbar](</charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/Time-Scale#time-frame-toolbar>) for more information.

* * *

### ticker

â¢ `Optional` **ticker** : `string`

It is an unique identifier for a particular symbol in your [symbology](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/Symbology>). If you specify this property, its value will be used for all data requests for this symbol. `ticker` will be treated the same as [LibrarySymbolInfo.name](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#name>) if not specified explicitly.

You should avoid using colons (":") in ticker values unless you are following the TradingView format: "NYSE:IBM". Using colons may cause unexpected behaviour and display bugs.

* * *

### timezone

â¢ **timezone** : [`Timezone`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#timezone>)

The time zone of the **exchange** where the symbol is listed. The time zone value should be in the OlsonDB format. Refer to [Time zones](</charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/timezones>) for a full list of supported time zones.

* * *

### type

â¢ **type** : `string`

Type of the instrument. Possible values: [SymbolType](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#symboltype>)

* * *

### unit_conversion_types

â¢ `Optional` **unit_conversion_types** : `string`[]

Allowed unit conversion group names.

* * *

### unit_id

â¢ `Optional` **unit_id** : `string`

A unique identifier of a unit in which the instrument is traded or some other identifier if unit conversion is enabled. It is displayed on the price axes.

* * *

### variable_tick_size

â¢ `Optional` **variable_tick_size** : `string`

Dynamic minimum price movement. It is used if the instrument's minimum price movement changes depending on the price range.

For example, '0.01 10 0.02 25 0.05', where the tick size is 0.01 for a price less than 10, the tick size is 0.02 for a price less than 25, the tick size is 0.05 for a price greater than or equal to 25.

* * *

### visible_plots_set

â¢ `Optional` **visible_plots_set** : [`VisiblePlotsSet`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#visibleplotsset>)

Represents what values are supported by the symbol. Possible values:

  * `ohlcv` â the symbol supports open, high, low, close prices and has volume.
  * `ohlc` â the symbol supports open, high, low, close, prices but doesn't have volume.
  * `c` â the symbol supports only close price. This makes the chart show the symbol data using only line-based styles.


**`Default`**
    
    
    'ohlcv'  
    

* * *

### volume_precision

â¢ `Optional` **volume_precision** : `number`

Integer showing typical volume value decimal places for a particular symbol. 0 means volume is always an integer. 1 means that there might be 1 numeric character after the comma.

**`Default`**
    
    
    '0'  
    

* * *

### weekly_multipliers

â¢ `Optional` **weekly_multipliers** : `string`[]

An array of weekly resolutions that your datafeed supports. Items in the array should be listed in ascending order and **should not** include letters, for example: `["1", "3"]`. This property is required to enable weekly resolutions. Refer to the [Configure resolutions in datafeed](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#resolution-in-weeks--months>) article for more information.

The library also uses resolutions listed in `weekly_multipliers` to display higher resolution that your datafeed does not explicitly support. If `weekly_multipliers` is not specified, the library cannot build additional resolutions.

**`Default`**
    
    
    ['1']


---

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.LibrarySymbolInfo

# Interface: LibrarySymbolInfo

[Datafeed](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Datafeed>).LibrarySymbolInfo

## Properties

### base_name

â¢ `Optional` **base_name** : `string`[]

Array of base symbols Example: for `AAPL*MSFT` it is `['NASDAQ:AAPL', 'NASDAQ:MSFT']`

* * *

### build_seconds_from_ticks

â¢ `Optional` **build_seconds_from_ticks** : `boolean`

The boolean value showing whether or not seconds bars for this symbol can be built from ticks. Only available in Trading Platform.

  * [LibrarySymbolInfo.has_seconds](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.LibrarySymbolInfo#has_seconds>) must also be `true`
  * [LibrarySymbolInfo.has_ticks](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.LibrarySymbolInfo#has_ticks>) must also be `true`
  * [LibrarySymbolInfo.seconds_multipliers](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.LibrarySymbolInfo#seconds_multipliers>) must be an empty array or only contain multipliers that the datafeed provides itself


The library builds resolutions from ticks only if there are no seconds resolutions from the datafeed or the provided resolutions are larger then the required one. For example, the datafeed provides `3S` resolution. In this case, the library can build only `1S` or `2S` resolutions from ticks. Resolutions such as `15S` will be build with seconds bars.

**`Default`**
    
    
    false  
    

* * *

### corrections

â¢ `Optional` **corrections** : `string`

List of corrections for a symbol. The corrections are days when the trading session differs from the default one set in [LibrarySymbolInfo.session](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.LibrarySymbolInfo#session>). The `corrections` value is a string in the following format: `SESSION:YYYYMMDD`. For more information, refer to [corrections](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/Symbology#corrections>).

The string below specifies corrections for two trading days:

  * November 13, 2018. This trading day is split into two sessions. The first session starts at 19:00 four days before (November 9, 2018) and ends at 23:50 four days before. The second session starts at 10:00 and ends at 18:45.
  * November 14, 2018. The session starts at 10:00 and ends at 14:00.


**`Example`**
    
    
    "1900F4-2350F4,1000-1845:20181113;1000-1400:20181114"  
    

* * *

### currency_code

â¢ `Optional` **currency_code** : `string`

The currency in which the instrument is traded or some other currency if currency conversion is enabled. It is displayed in the Security Info dialog and on the price axes.

* * *

### daily_multipliers

â¢ `Optional` **daily_multipliers** : `string`[]

An array of daily resolutions that your datafeed supports. Items in the array should be listed in ascending order and **should not** include letters, for example: `["1", "2"]`. This property is required to enable daily resolutions. Refer to the [Configure resolutions in datafeed](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#resolution-in-days>) article for more information.

The library also uses resolutions listed in `daily_multipliers` to display higher resolution that your datafeed does not explicitly support. If `daily_multipliers` is not specified, the library cannot build additional resolutions.

**`Default`**
    
    
    ["1"]  
    

* * *

### data_status

â¢ `Optional` **data_status** : `"streaming"` | `"endofday"` | `"delayed_streaming"`

The status code of a series with this symbol. For `delayed_streaming` and `endofday` type of data, the status is displayed as an icon and the _Data is delayed_ section in the [_Legend_](</charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/Legend#display-delayed-data-information>), next to the market status icon. Note that you should also enable the [`display_data_mode`](</charting-library-docs/latest/customization/Featuresets#display_data_mode>) featureset.

When declaring `delayed_streaming` you also have to specify its [LibrarySymbolInfo.delay](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.LibrarySymbolInfo#delay>) in seconds.

* * *

### delay

â¢ `Optional` **delay** : `number`

Type of delay that is associated to the data or real delay for real time data.

  * `0` for realtime
  * `-1` for endofday
  * or delay in seconds (for delayed realtime)


* * *

### description

â¢ **description** : `string`

The description of the symbol. Will be displayed in the chart legend for this symbol.

* * *

### exchange

â¢ **exchange** : `string`

Traded exchange (current (proxy) exchange). The name will be displayed in the chart legend for this symbol.

**`Example`**
    
    
    "NYSE"  
    

* * *

### exchange_logo

â¢ `Optional` **exchange_logo** : `string`

URL of image to be displayed as the logo for the exchange. The `show_exchange_logos` featureset needs to be enabled for this to be visible in the UI.

The image should ideally be square in dimension. You can use any image type which the browser supports natively. Simple SVG images are recommended.

Examples:

  * `https://yourserver.com/exchangeLogo.svg`
  * `/images/myImage.png`
  * `data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3...`
  * `data:image/jpeg;base64,/9j/4AAQSkZJRgABAQAASABIAAD/4...`


* * *

### expiration_date

â¢ `Optional` **expiration_date** : `number`

Unix timestamp of the expiration date. One must set this value when `expired` = `true`. The library will request data for this symbol starting from that time point.

* * *

### expired

â¢ `Optional` **expired** : `boolean`

Boolean showing whether this symbol is expired futures contract or not.

**`Default`**
    
    
    false  
    

* * *

### format

â¢ **format** : [`SeriesFormat`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Datafeed#seriesformat>)

Format of displaying labels on the price scale:

`price` \- formats decimal or fractional numbers based on `minmov`, `pricescale`, `minmove2`, `fractional` and `variableMinTick` values. See [Price format](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/Symbology#price-format>) for more details. `volume` \- formats decimal numbers in thousands, millions, billions or trillions

* * *

### fractional

â¢ `Optional` **fractional** : `boolean`

For common prices this can be skipped.

Fractional prices are displayed 2 different forms: 1) `xx'yy` (for example, `133'21`) 2) `xx'yy'zz` (for example, `133'21'5`).

  * `xx` is an integer part.
  * `minmov/pricescale` is a Fraction.
  * `minmove2` is used in form 2.
  * `fractional` is `true`.
  * `variableMinTick` is skipped.


Example:

If `minmov = 1`, `pricescale = 128` and `minmove2 = 4`:

  * `119'16'0` represents `119 + 16/32`
  * `119'16'2` represents `119 + 16.25/32`
  * `119'16'5` represents `119 + 16.5/32`
  * `119'16'7` represents `119 + 16.75/32`


More examples:

  * `ZBM2014 (T-Bond)` with `1/32`: `minmov = 1`, `pricescale = 32`, `minmove2 = 0`
  * `ZCM2014 (Corn)` with `2/8`: `minmov = 2`, `pricescale = 8`, `minmove2 = 0`
  * `ZFM2014 (5 year t-note)` with `1/4 of 1/32`: `minmov = 1`, `pricescale = 128`, `minmove2 = 4`


* * *

### has_daily

â¢ `Optional` **has_daily** : `boolean`

A flag indicating whether your datafeed contains daily data for this symbol. If `true`, the library requests this data when a daily resolution is selected. If `false`, _No data here_ is displayed on the chart.

`has_daily` is set to `true` by default. However, you should also specify [daily_multipliers](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.LibrarySymbolInfo#daily_multipliers>) to enable daily resolutions. Refer to the [Configure resolutions in datafeed](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#resolution-in-days>) article for more information.

**`Default`**
    
    
    true  
    

* * *

### has_empty_bars

â¢ `Optional` **has_empty_bars** : `boolean`

The boolean value showing whether the library should generate empty bars in the session when there is no data from the data feed for this particular time.

I.e., if your session is `0900-1600` and your data has gaps between `11:00` and `12:00` and your `has_empty_bars` is `true`, then the Library will fill the gaps with bars for this time.

Flag `has_empty_bars` = `true` cannot be used if featureset `disable_resolution_rebuild` is enabled.

**`Default`**
    
    
    false  
    

* * *

### has_intraday

â¢ `Optional` **has_intraday** : `boolean`

A flag indicating whether your datafeed contains intraday (minutes) data for this symbol. If `true`, the library requests this data when an intraday resolution is selected. If `false`, _No data here_ is displayed on the chart.

This property is required to enable intraday resolutions. Refer to the [Configure resolutions in datafeed](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#resolution-in-minutes-intraday>) article for more information.

**`Default`**
    
    
    false  
    

* * *

### has_seconds

â¢ `Optional` **has_seconds** : `boolean`

A flag indicating whether your datafeed contains seconds data for this symbol. If `true`, the library requests this data when a seconds resolution is selected. If `false`, _No data here_ is displayed on the chart.

You should set `has_seconds` to `true` to enable seconds resolutions. Refer to the [Configure resolutions in datafeed](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#resolution-in-seconds>) article for more information.

**`Default`**
    
    
    false  
    

* * *

### has_ticks

â¢ `Optional` **has_ticks** : `boolean`

A flag indicating whether your datafeed contains ticks data for this symbol. If `true`, the library requests this data when a resolution in ticks is selected. If `false`, _No data here_ is displayed on the chart.

You should set `has_ticks` to `true` to enable ticks resolutions. Refer to the [Configure resolutions in datafeed](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#resolution-in-ticks>) article for more information.

**`Default`**
    
    
    false  
    

* * *

### has_weekly_and_monthly

â¢ `Optional` **has_weekly_and_monthly** : `boolean`

A flag indicating whether your datafeed contains weekly or monthly data for this symbol. If `true`, the library requests this data when the corresponding resolution is selected. To enable weekly or monthly resolutions, you should also specify the [weekly_multipliers](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.LibrarySymbolInfo#weekly_multipliers>) or [monthly_multipliers](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.LibrarySymbolInfo#monthly_multipliers>) properties. Refer to the [Configure resolutions in datafeed](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#resolution-in-weeks--months>) article for more information.

If `has_weekly_and_monthly` is set to `false`, the library attempts to build the resolutions using daily bars. Note that building bars requires a large number of requests to your datafeed. If the library fails to build bars, _No data here_ is displayed on the chart.

**`Default`**
    
    
    false  
    

* * *

### industry

â¢ `Optional` **industry** : `string`

Industry for stocks to be displayed in the Security Info.

* * *

### intraday_multipliers

â¢ `Optional` **intraday_multipliers** : `string`[]

An array of intraday (minutes) resolutions that your datafeed supports. Items in the array should be listed in ascending order, for example: `["1", "2"]`.

This property is required to enable intraday resolutions. Refer to the [Configure resolutions in datafeed](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#resolution-in-minutes-intraday>) article for more information. Note that each resolution in `intraday_multipliers` should be handled in the [IDatafeedChartApi.getBars](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.IDatafeedChartApi#getbars>) implementation. Consider the [example](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#example>).

The library also uses resolutions listed in `intraday_multipliers` to display higher resolution that your datafeed does not explicitly support. If `intraday_multipliers` is not specified, the library cannot build additional resolutions.

Note that the library **cannot** build daily, weekly, or monthly resolutions using intraday data.

**`Default`**
    
    
    [] â specifies that the datafeed can provide data for any requested resolution.  
    

* * *

### library_custom_fields

â¢ `Optional` **library_custom_fields** : `Record`<`string`, `unknown`>

Additional metadata to include with the symbol information. These parameters will not affect existing properties such as 'ticker' or 'name' and will not be processed by the library. However, they can be accessed later through the IChartWidgetApi.symbolExt method, impacting existing properties such as 'ticker' or 'name'.

* * *

### listed_exchange

â¢ **listed_exchange** : `string`

short name of the exchange where this symbol is traded (real listed exchange). The name will be displayed in the chart legend for this symbol.

**`Example`**
    
    
    "NYSE"  
    

* * *

### logo_urls

â¢ `Optional` **logo_urls** : [`string`, `string`] | [`string`]

URL of image/s to be displayed as the logo/s for the symbol. The `show_symbol_logos` featureset needs to be enabled for this to be visible in the UI.

  * If a single url is returned then that url will solely be used to display the symbol logo.
  * If two urls are provided then the images will be displayed as two partially overlapping circles with the first url appearing on top. This is typically used for FOREX where you would like to display two country flags are the symbol logo.


The image/s should ideally be square in dimension. You can use any image type which the browser supports natively.

Examples:

  * `https://yourserver.com/apple.svg`
  * `/images/myImage.png`
  * `data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3...`
  * `data:image/jpeg;base64,/9j/4AAQSkZJRgABAQAASABIAAD/4...`


* * *

### long_description

â¢ `Optional` **long_description** : `string`

Symbol Long description

Optional long(er) description for the symbol.

* * *

### minmov

â¢ **minmov** : `number`

The number of units that make up one tick. For example, U.S. equities are quotes in decimals, and tick in decimals, and can go up +/- `.01`. Therefore, the tick increment is 1 and `minmov = 1`. But the e-mini S&P futures contract, though quoted in decimals, goes up in `.25` increments, so the tick increment is 25 and `minmov = 25`. Refer to [Price format](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/Symbology#price-format>) for more information.

* * *

### minmove2

â¢ `Optional` **minmove2** : `number`

This property is used to display prices in the [fraction of a fraction](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/Symbology#fraction-of-a-fraction-format>) format. For example, the ZBM2023 tick size is 1/4 of a 32nd. To display this security, set `minmov = 1`, `pricescale = 128`, `minmove2 = 4`.

For common prices, `minmove2` can be skipped or set to `0`.

* * *

### monthly_multipliers

â¢ `Optional` **monthly_multipliers** : `string`[]

An array of monthly resolutions that your datafeed supports. Items in the array should be listed in ascending order and **should not** include letters, for example: `["1", "3", "6", "12"]`. This property is required to enable monthly resolutions. Refer to the [Configure resolutions in datafeed](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#resolution-in-weeks--months>) article for more information.

The library also uses resolutions listed in `monthly_multipliers` to display higher resolution that your datafeed does not explicitly support. If `monthly_multipliers` is not specified, the library cannot build additional resolutions.

**`Default`**
    
    
    ['1']  
    

* * *

### name

â¢ **name** : `string`

It is a symbol name within an exchange, such as `AAPL` or `9988` (Hong Kong). Note that it should not contain the exchange name. This symbol name is visible to users and can be repeated.

By default, `name` is used to resolve symbols in the [Datafeed API](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/datafeed-api/>). If you use [LibrarySymbolInfo.ticker](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.LibrarySymbolInfo#ticker>), the library will use the ticker for Datafeed API requests.

* * *

### original_currency_code

â¢ `Optional` **original_currency_code** : `string`

The currency in which the instrument is traded.

* * *

### original_unit_id

â¢ `Optional` **original_unit_id** : `string`

A unique identifier of a unit in which the instrument is traded.

* * *

### price_source_id

â¢ `Optional` **price_source_id** : `string`

Optional ID of a price source for a symbol. Should match one of the price sources from the [price_sources](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.LibrarySymbolInfo#price_sources>) array.

Note that you should set the [`symbol_info_price_source`](</charting-library-docs/latest/customization/Featuresets#symbol_info_price_source>) featureset to `true` to display the symbol price source in the main series legend.

* * *

### price_sources

â¢ `Optional` **price_sources** : [`SymbolInfoPriceSource`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.SymbolInfoPriceSource>)[]

Supported price sources for the symbol. Price sources appear in the series legend and indicate the origin of values represented by symbol bars. Example price sources: "Spot Price", "Ask", "Bid", etc. The price source information is valuable when viewing non-OHLC series types.

Note that you should set the [`symbol_info_price_source`](</charting-library-docs/latest/customization/Featuresets#symbol_info_price_source>) featureset to `true` to display the symbol price source in the main series legend.

**`Example`**
    
    
    [{ id: '1', name: 'Spot Price' }, { id: '321', name: 'Bid' }]  
    

* * *

### pricescale

â¢ **pricescale** : `number`

A number of decimal places or fractions that the price has.

The `pricescale` value depends on the [price format](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/Symbology#price-format>). If you want to display the price in the decimal format, `pricescale` should be `10^n`, where `n` is the number of decimal places. **Examples:** `1`, `10`, `10000000`.

If you want to display the price in the fractional format, `pricescale` should be `2^n`. This value represents the number of fractions. **Examples:** `8`, `16`, `256`.

* * *

### seconds_multipliers

â¢ `Optional` **seconds_multipliers** : `string`[]

An array of seconds resolutions that your datafeed supports. Items in the array should be listed in ascending order and **should not** include letters, for example: `["1", "2"]`. This property is required to enable seconds resolutions. Refer to the [Configure resolutions in datafeed](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#resolution-in-seconds>) article for more information.

The library also uses resolutions listed in `seconds_multipliers` to display higher resolution that your datafeed does not explicitly support. If `seconds_multipliers` is not specified, the library cannot build additional resolutions. Consider the example. You need to enable one-second and five-second resolutions but your datafeed contains only one-second data. In this case, you should set `seconds_multipliers` to `["1"]`. The library will build the five-second resolution from one-second data.

* * *

### sector

â¢ `Optional` **sector** : `string`

Sector for stocks to be displayed in the Security Info.

* * *

### session

â¢ **session** : `string`

Trading hours for the symbol. The time should be in the **exchange** time zone specified in the [LibrarySymbolInfo.timezone](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.LibrarySymbolInfo#timezone>) property. Refer to the [Trading sessions](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/Trading-Sessions>) article for more information.

**`Example`**
    
    
    "1700-0200"  
    

* * *

### session_display

â¢ `Optional` **session_display** : `string`

The session value to display in the UI. If not specified, then `session` is used.

* * *

### session_holidays

â¢ `Optional` **session_holidays** : `string`

A string that contains a list of non-trading holidays for the symbol. Holiday dates should be in the `YYYYMMDD` format. These dates are not displayed on the chart.

You can specify a correction for a holiday using [LibrarySymbolInfo.corrections](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.LibrarySymbolInfo#corrections>).

**`Example`**
    
    
    "20181105,20181107,20181112"  
    

* * *

### subsession_id

â¢ `Optional` **subsession_id** : `string`

An ID of a subsession specified in [subsessions](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.LibrarySymbolInfo#subsessions>). The value must match the subsession that is currently displayed on the chart. For more information, refer to the [Extended sessions](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/Symbology#extended-sessions>) section.

* * *

### subsessions

â¢ `Optional` **subsessions** : [`LibrarySubsessionInfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.LibrarySubsessionInfo>)[]

An array of objects that contain information about certain subsessions within the extended session. For more information, refer to the [Extended sessions](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/Symbology#extended-sessions>) section.

* * *

### supported_resolutions

â¢ `Optional` **supported_resolutions** : [`ResolutionString`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Datafeed#resolutionstring>)[]

An array of [resolutions](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions>) which should be enabled in the _Resolution_ drop-down menu for this symbol. Each item of the array is expected to be a string that has a specific [format](</charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/Resolution#resolution-format>).

If one changes the symbol and the new symbol does not support the selected resolution, an error message will be shown on the chart.

**Resolution availability logic (pseudocode):**
    
    
    resolutionAvailable  =  
        resolution.isIntraday  
            ? symbol.has_intraday && symbol.supported_resolutions(resolution)  
            : symbol.supported_resolutions(resolution);  
    

If `supported_resolutions` is `[]` (empty array), all resolutions are disabled in the _Resolution_ drop-down menu.

If `supported_resolutions` is `undefined`, all resolutions that the chart support ([DatafeedConfiguration.supported_resolutions](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.DatafeedConfiguration#supported_resolutions>)) and custom resolutions are enabled.

Note that the list of available time frames depends on supported resolutions. Time frames that require resolutions that are unavailable for a particular symbol will be hidden. Refer to [Time frame toolbar](</charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/Time-Scale#time-frame-toolbar>) for more information.

* * *

### ticker

â¢ `Optional` **ticker** : `string`

It is an unique identifier for a particular symbol in your [symbology](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/Symbology>). If you specify this property, its value will be used for all data requests for this symbol. `ticker` will be treated the same as [LibrarySymbolInfo.name](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.LibrarySymbolInfo#name>) if not specified explicitly.

You should avoid using colons (":") in ticker values unless you are following the TradingView format: "NYSE:IBM". Using colons may cause unexpected behaviour and display bugs.

* * *

### timezone

â¢ **timezone** : [`Timezone`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Datafeed#timezone>)

The time zone of the **exchange** where the symbol is listed. The time zone value should be in the OlsonDB format. Refer to [Time zones](</charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/timezones>) for a full list of supported time zones.

* * *

### type

â¢ **type** : `string`

Type of the instrument. Possible values: SymbolType

* * *

### unit_conversion_types

â¢ `Optional` **unit_conversion_types** : `string`[]

Allowed unit conversion group names.

* * *

### unit_id

â¢ `Optional` **unit_id** : `string`

A unique identifier of a unit in which the instrument is traded or some other identifier if unit conversion is enabled. It is displayed on the price axes.

* * *

### variable_tick_size

â¢ `Optional` **variable_tick_size** : `string`

Dynamic minimum price movement. It is used if the instrument's minimum price movement changes depending on the price range.

For example, '0.01 10 0.02 25 0.05', where the tick size is 0.01 for a price less than 10, the tick size is 0.02 for a price less than 25, the tick size is 0.05 for a price greater than or equal to 25.

* * *

### visible_plots_set

â¢ `Optional` **visible_plots_set** : [`VisiblePlotsSet`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Datafeed#visibleplotsset>)

Represents what values are supported by the symbol. Possible values:

  * `ohlcv` â the symbol supports open, high, low, close prices and has volume.
  * `ohlc` â the symbol supports open, high, low, close, prices but doesn't have volume.
  * `c` â the symbol supports only close price. This makes the chart show the symbol data using only line-based styles.


**`Default`**
    
    
    'ohlcv'  
    

* * *

### volume_precision

â¢ `Optional` **volume_precision** : `number`

Integer showing typical volume value decimal places for a particular symbol. 0 means volume is always an integer. 1 means that there might be 1 numeric character after the comma.

**`Default`**
    
    
    '0'  
    

* * *

### weekly_multipliers

â¢ `Optional` **weekly_multipliers** : `string`[]

An array of weekly resolutions that your datafeed supports. Items in the array should be listed in ascending order and **should not** include letters, for example: `["1", "3"]`. This property is required to enable weekly resolutions. Refer to the [Configure resolutions in datafeed](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#resolution-in-weeks--months>) article for more information.

The library also uses resolutions listed in `weekly_multipliers` to display higher resolution that your datafeed does not explicitly support. If `weekly_multipliers` is not specified, the library cannot build additional resolutions.

**`Default`**
    
    
    ['1']


---

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LineBreakStylePreferences

# Interface: LineBreakStylePreferences

[Charting Library](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library>).LineBreakStylePreferences

## Properties

### borderDownColor

â¢ **borderDownColor** : `string`

Down bar border color

* * *

### borderDownColorProjection

â¢ **borderDownColorProjection** : `string`

Projection down bar border color

* * *

### borderUpColor

â¢ **borderUpColor** : `string`

Up bar border color

* * *

### borderUpColorProjection

â¢ **borderUpColorProjection** : `string`

Projection up bar border color

* * *

### downColor

â¢ **downColor** : `string`

Down bar color

* * *

### downColorProjection

â¢ **downColorProjection** : `string`

Projection down bar color

* * *

### upColor

â¢ **upColor** : `string`

Up bar color

* * *

### upColorProjection

â¢ **upColorProjection** : `string`

Projection up bar color


---

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LineStylePreferences

# Interface: LineStylePreferences

[Charting Library](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library>).LineStylePreferences

## Properties

### color

â¢ **color** : `string`

Line color

* * *

### colorType

â¢ `Optional` **colorType** : [`ColorType`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.ColorType>)

Color type [ColorType](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.ColorType>)

* * *

### gradientEndColor

â¢ `Optional` **gradientEndColor** : `string`

Line gradient end color

* * *

### gradientStartColor

â¢ `Optional` **gradientStartColor** : `string`

Line gradient start color

* * *

### linestyle

â¢ **linestyle** : `number`

Line Style [LineStyle](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.LineStyle>)

### linewidth

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.Mark

# Interface: Mark

[Charting Library](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library>).Mark

## Properties

### borderWidth

â¢ `Optional` **borderWidth** : `number`

Border Width

* * *

### color

â¢ **color** : [`MarkConstColors`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#markconstcolors>) | [`MarkCustomColor`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.MarkCustomColor>)

Color for the mark

* * *

### hoveredBorderWidth

â¢ `Optional` **hoveredBorderWidth** : `number`

Border Width when hovering over bar mark

* * *

### id

â¢ **id** : `string` | `number`

ID of the mark

* * *

### imageUrl

â¢ `Optional` **imageUrl** : `string`

Optional URL for an image to be displayed within the timescale mark.

The image should ideally be square in dimension. You can use any image type which the browser supports natively.

Examples:

  * `https://yourserver.com/adobe.svg`
  * `/images/myImage.png`
  * `data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3...`
  * `data:image/jpeg;base64,/9j/4AAQSkZJRgABAQAASABIAAD/4...`


* * *

### label

â¢ **label** : `string`

Label for the mark

* * *

### labelFontColor

â¢ **labelFontColor** : `string`

Text color for the mark

* * *

### minSize

â¢ **minSize** : `number`

Minimum size for the mark

* * *

### showLabelWhenImageLoaded

â¢ `Optional` **showLabelWhenImageLoaded** : `boolean`

Continue to show text label even when an image has been loaded for the timescale mark.

Defaults to `false` if undefined.

* * *

### text

â¢ **text** : `string`

Text content for the mark

* * *

### time

â¢ **time** : `number`

Time for the mark. Unix timestamp in seconds.


---

### clientX

â¢ **clientX** : `number`

X (horizontal) coordinate (in pixels) at which the mouse event occurred, relative to the left edge of the applications viewport.

* * *

### clientY

â¢ **clientY** : `number`

Y (vertical) coordinate (in pixels) at which the mouse event occurred, relative to the left edge of the applications viewport.

* * *

### pageX

â¢ **pageX** : `number`

X (horizontal) coordinate (in pixels) at which the mouse event occurred, relative to the left edge of the entire document.

* * *

### pageY

â¢ **pageY** : `number`

Y (vertical) coordinate (in pixels) at which the mouse event occurred, relative to the left edge of the entire document.

* * *

### screenX

â¢ **screenX** : `number`

X (horizontal) coordinate (in pixels) at which the mouse event occurred, in global screen coordinates.

* * *

### screenY

â¢ **screenY** : `number`

Y (vertical) coordinate (in pixels) at which the mouse event occurred, in global screen coordinates.

[Charting Library](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library>).NewsItem

## Properties

### fullDescription

â¢ `Optional` **fullDescription** : `string`

Full description

* * *

### link

â¢ `Optional` **link** : `string`

Link to the news item. This link is opened in a pop-up dialog when a user clicks on the corresponding news item. If `link` is not provided, the information from the [fullDescription](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.NewsItem#fulldescription>) property is displayed instead.

* * *

### provider

â¢ **provider** : [`NewsProvider`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.NewsProvider>)

Provider of the news item

### published

â¢ **published** : `number`

Published date

* * *

### shortDescription

â¢ `Optional` **shortDescription** : `string`

Short description

* * *

### title

â¢ **title** : `string`

News item title.

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.NumericFormattingParams

# Interface: NumericFormattingParams

[Charting Library](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library>).NumericFormattingParams

Formatting options for numbers

## Properties

### decimal_sign

â¢ **decimal_sign** : `string`

String that represents the decimal part of a number. This character separates the integer part from the fractional part.

**`Example`**
    
    
    "123.4" // Using a dot as the decimal sign  
    

**`Example`**
    
    
    "123,4" // Using a comma as the decimal sign  
    

**`Example`**
    
    
    "123'4" // Using an apostrophe as the decimal sign  
    

* * *

### grouping_separator

â¢ **grouping_separator** : `string`

String that represents the grouping separator for large numbers. This character separates groups of thousands to improve readability.

**`Example`**
    
    
    "1 234.5" // Using a space as the grouping separator  
    

**`Example`**
    
    
    "1,234.5" // Using a comma as the grouping separator


---

* * *

* * *

### smoothed ma.linestyle

â¢ **smoothed ma.linestyle** : `number`

* * *

Default value: `1`

* * *

### smoothed ma.plottype

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.OrderDialogOptions

# Interface: OrderDialogOptions

[Broker](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker>).OrderDialogOptions

## Hierarchy

  * [`TradingDialogOptions`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.TradingDialogOptions>)

â³ **`OrderDialogOptions`**


## Properties

### customFields

â¢ `Optional` **customFields** : [`TradingDialogCustomField`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker#tradingdialogcustomfield>)[]

Custom fields that are displayed in the Order Ticket. Refer to the [Add custom fields](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/order-ticket#add-custom-fields>) section for more information.

**Example**
    
    
    customFields: [  
        {  
            inputType: 'TextWithCheckBox',  
            id: '2410',  
            title: 'Digital Signature',  
            placeHolder: 'Enter your personal digital signature',  
            value: {  
                text: '',  
                checked: false,  
            },  
            customInfo: {  
                asterix: true,  
                checkboxTitle: 'Save',  
            },  
        }  
    ]  
    

#### Inherited from

[TradingDialogOptions](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.TradingDialogOptions>).[customFields](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.TradingDialogOptions#customfields>)

* * *

### maxExitLevelsCount

â¢ `Optional` **maxExitLevelsCount** : `number`

Specifies the maximum number of [exit levels](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/brackets#multiple-exit-levels>) (brackets) that can be attached to a single parent order or position.

#### Inherited from

[TradingDialogOptions](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.TradingDialogOptions>).[maxExitLevelsCount](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.TradingDialogOptions#maxexitlevelscount>)

* * *

### showTotal

â¢ `Optional` **showTotal** : `boolean`

Using this flag, you can change `Trade Value` to `Total` in the _Order Info_ section of the [Order Ticket](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/order-ticket>).


---

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.OrderDurationMetaInfo

# Interface: OrderDurationMetaInfo

[Broker](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker>).OrderDurationMetaInfo

Order duration options that determine how long the order remains active. Refer to [Enable Time in force menu](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/order-ticket#enable-time-in-force-menu>) for more information.

## Properties

### default

â¢ `Optional` **default** : `boolean`

Default duration. Only one duration object in the durations array can have a `true` value for this field. The default duration will be used when the user places orders in the silent mode and it will be the selected one when the user opens Order Ticket for the first time.

* * *

### hasDatePicker

â¢ `Optional` **hasDatePicker** : `boolean`

If it is set to `true`, then the Display date control in Order Ticket for this duration type will be displayed.

* * *

### hasTimePicker

â¢ `Optional` **hasTimePicker** : `boolean`

If it is set to `true`, then the Display time control in Order Ticket for this duration type will be displayed.

* * *

### name

â¢ **name** : `string`

Localized title of the duration. The title will be displayed in the Duration control of Order Ticket.

* * *

### supportedOrderTypes

â¢ `Optional` **supportedOrderTypes** : [`OrderType`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Broker.OrderType>)[]

A list of order types for which this duration type will be displayed in the Duration control of Order Ticket. Default value is `[OrderType.Limit, OrderType.Stop, OrderType.StopLimit]`.

* * *

### value

â¢ **value** : `string`

Duration identifier


---

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.OrderTicketSettings

# Interface: OrderTicketSettings

[Broker](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker>).OrderTicketSettings

Settings for the ellipsis menu of the [Order Ticket](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/order-ticket>). You can read and adjust these settings using the [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.getOrderTicketSetting](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#getorderticketsetting>) and [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.setOrderTicketSetting](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#setorderticketsetting>) methods.

## Properties

### showOrderPreview

â¢ **showOrderPreview** : `boolean`

If true, the "Show order confirmations" setting is displayed.

* * *

### showRelativePriceControl

â¢ **showRelativePriceControl** : `boolean`

If true, the "Show Order Price in Ticks" setting is displayed.


---

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.OverlayIndicatorOverrides

# Interface: OverlayIndicatorOverrides

[Charting Library](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library>).OverlayIndicatorOverrides

## Properties

### allowExtendTimeScale

â¢ **allowExtendTimeScale** : `boolean`

Enables the _Overlay_ to extend the time scale. Default value: `false`

* * *

### minTick

â¢ **minTick** : `string`

[`minTick`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ChartPropertiesOverrides#mainseriespropertiesmintick>) Default value: `default`

* * *

### showPriceLine

â¢ **showPriceLine** : `boolean`

Price line visibility. Default value: `false`

* * *

### style

â¢ **style** : [`Bar`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.ChartStyle#bar>) | [`Candle`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.ChartStyle#candle>) | [`Line`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.ChartStyle#line>) | [`Area`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.ChartStyle#area>) | [`HollowCandle`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.ChartStyle#hollowcandle>) | [`Baseline`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.ChartStyle#baseline>) | [`HiLo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.ChartStyle#hilo>) | [`Column`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.ChartStyle#column>) | [`LineWithMarkers`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.ChartStyle#linewithmarkers>) | [`Stepline`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.ChartStyle#stepline>) | [`HLCArea`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.ChartStyle#hlcarea>) | [`HLCBars`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.ChartStyle#hlcbars>)

The style type for _Overlay_ can be any of the following. Default value: 2 (`line`)

For each style, there are additional properties that you can customize. Refer to the links in the _OverlayIndicatorOverrides_ column to see the lists of properties.

ChartStyle name| ChartStyle #| OverlayIndicatorOverrides| Bar| 0| [OverlayIndicatorOverridesBar](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.OverlayIndicatorOverridesBar>)| Candle| 1| [OverlayIndicatorOverridesCandle](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.OverlayIndicatorOverridesCandle>)| Line| 2| [OverlayIndicatorOverridesLine](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.OverlayIndicatorOverridesLine>)| Area| 3| [OverlayIndicatorOverridesArea](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.OverlayIndicatorOverridesArea>)| HollowCandle| 4| [OverlayIndicatorOverridesHollowCandle](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.OverlayIndicatorOverridesHollowCandle>)| Baseline| 5| [OverlayIndicatorOverridesBaseline](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.OverlayIndicatorOverridesBaseline>)| HiLo| 6| [OverlayIndicatorOverridesHiLo](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.OverlayIndicatorOverridesHiLo>)| Column| 7| [OverlayIndicatorOverridesColumn](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.OverlayIndicatorOverridesColumn>)| HLCArea| 8| [OverlayIndicatorOverridesHLCArea](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.OverlayIndicatorOverridesHLCArea>)| LineWithMarkers| 14| [OverlayIndicatorOverridesLineWithMarkers](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.OverlayIndicatorOverridesLineWithMarkers>)| Stepline| 15| [OverlayIndicatorOverridesStepline](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.OverlayIndicatorOverridesStepline>)  
---|---|---

## Indexable

âª [key: `string`]: [`StudyOverrideValueType`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#studyoverridevaluetype>) | `undefined`

Use the properties as follow: `areaStyle.color1`: `pink`

## Properties

### color1

â¢ `Optional` **color1** : `string`

Top color of the gradient fill. Must be an RGBA color string (e.g., `"rgba(255, 0, 0, 0.5)"`).

#### Inherited from

Partial.color1

* * *

### color2

â¢ `Optional` **color2** : `string`

Bottom color of the gradient fill. Must be an RGBA color string (e.g., `"rgba(255, 0, 0, 0.5)"`).

#### Inherited from

Partial.color2

* * *

### linecolor

â¢ `Optional` **linecolor** : `string`

Line color. Must be a simple color string (e.g., `"red"`, `"#FF0000"`).

#### Inherited from

Partial.linecolor

* * *

### linestyle

â¢ `Optional` **linestyle** : `number`

Line Style [LineStyle](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.LineStyle>)

#### Inherited from

Partial.linestyle

* * *

### linewidth

â¢ `Optional` **linewidth** : `number`

Line width

#### Inherited from

Partial.linewidth

* * *

### style

â¢ **style** : [`Area`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.ChartStyle#area>)

* * *

### transparency

â¢ `Optional` **transparency** : `number`

Transparency. Range [0..100] `0` \- fully opaque, `100` \- fully transparent.

**Note** : Rather use `rgba` color string for setting transparency.

#### Inherited from

Partial.transparency

[Charting Library](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library>).ParabolicSARIndicatorOverrides

Overrides for the 'Parabolic SAR' indicator.

Use these properties to customize indicator via [IChartWidgetApi.createStudy](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#createstudy>) and [IStudyApi.applyOverrides](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IStudyApi#applyoverrides>).

### plot.linewidth

Default value: `false`

* * *

### plot.transparency

Default value: `0`

# Interface: PineJSStd

[Charting Library](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library>).PineJSStd

PineJS standard library functions.

## Properties

### isZero

#### Type declaration

â¸ (`v`): `number`

Check if a value is zero.

##### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `v`| `number`| the value to test.

`number`

`true` if the value is zero, `false` otherwise.

â¢ **max_series_default_size** : `10001`

### abs

Absolute value of x is x if x >= 0, or -x otherwise.

Name| Type| `x`| `number`  
---|---  
  
#### Returns

`number`

The absolute value of `x`

* * *

### accdist

Accumulation/distribution index.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `context`| [`IContext`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IContext>)| PineJS execution context.  
---|---|---

`number`

Accumulation/distribution index.

* * *

### acos

â¸ **acos**(`x`): `number`

The acos function returns the arccosine (in radians) of number such that `cos(acos(y)) = y` for `y` in range `[-1, 1]`.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `x`| `number`| Angle, in radians.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`number`

The arc cosine of a value; the returned angle is in the range `[0, Pi]`, or na if y is outside of range `[-1, 1]`.

* * *

### add_days_considering_dst

â¸ **add_days_considering_dst**(`timezone`, `utcTime`, `daysCount`): `Date`

Get time in `daysCount` number of days while taking Daylight savings time into account.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `timezone`| `string`| Timezone| `utcTime`| `Date`| Date (JS built-in)| `daysCount`| `number`| Number of days  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`Date`

The time is `daysCount` number of days, taking into account Daylight savings time.

* * *

### add_years_considering_dst

â¸ **add_years_considering_dst**(`timezone`, `utcTime`, `yearsCount`): `Date`

Get time in `yearsCount` number of years while taking Daylight savings time into account.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `timezone`| `string`| Timezone| `utcTime`| `Date`| Date (JS built-in)| `yearsCount`| `number`| Number of years  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`Date`

The time is `yearsCount` number of years, taking into account Daylight savings time.

* * *

### alma

â¸ **alma**(`series`, `length`, `offset`, `sigma`): `number`

Arnaud Legoux Moving Average. It uses Gaussian distribution as weights for moving average.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `series`| [`IPineSeries`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IPineSeries>)| Series of values to process.| `length`| `number`| Number of bars (length).| `offset`| `number`| Controls tradeoff between smoothness (closer to 1) and responsiveness (closer to 0).| `sigma`| `number`| Changes the smoothness of ALMA. The larger sigma the smoother ALMA.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`number`

* * *

### and

â¸ **and**(`n_0`, `n_1`): `number`

Logical AND.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| `n_0`| `number`| `n_1`| `number`  
---|---  
  
#### Returns

`number`

`1` if both values are truthy, `0` otherwise.

* * *

### asin

â¸ **asin**(`x`): `number`

The asin function returns the arcsine (in radians) of number such that `sin(asin(y)) = y` for `y` in range `[-1, 1]`.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `x`| `number`| Angle, in radians.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`number`

The arcsine of a value; the returned angle is in the range `[-Pi/2, Pi/2]`, or na if y is outside of range `[-1, 1]`.

* * *

### atan

â¸ **atan**(`x`): `number`

The atan function returns the arctangent (in radians) of number such that `tan(atan(y)) = y` for any `y`.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `x`| `number`| Angle, in radians.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`number`

The arc tangent of a value; the returned angle is in the range `[-Pi/2, Pi/2]`.

* * *

### atr

â¸ **atr**(`length`, `context`): `number`

Function atr (average true range) returns the RMA of true range. True range is `max(high - low, abs(high - close[1]), abs(low - close[1]))`

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `length`| `number`| Length (number of bars back).| `context`| [`IContext`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IContext>)| PineJS execution context.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`number`

Average true range.

### dmi

â¸ **dmi**(`diLength`, `adxSmoothingLength`, `context`): [`number`, `number`, `number`, `number`, `number`]

Calculates the directional movement values +DI, -DI, DX, ADX, and ADXR.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `diLength`| `number`| Number of bars (length) used when calculating the +DI and -DI values.| `adxSmoothingLength`| `number`| Number of bars (length) used when calculating the ADX value.| `context`| [`IContext`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IContext>)| PineJS execution context.  
---|---|---

[`number`, `number`, `number`, `number`, `number`]

An array of the +DI, -DI, DX, ADX, and ADXR values with diLength smoothing for the (+/-)DI values and adxSmoothingLength for the ADX value.

* * *

### ema

â¸ **ema**(`source`, `length`, `context`): `number`

Exponential Moving Average. In EMA weighting factors decrease exponentially.

It calculates by using a formula: `EMA = alpha * x + (1 - alpha) * EMA[1]`, where `alpha = 2 / (y + 1)`.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `source`| [`IPineSeries`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IPineSeries>)| Series of values to process.| `length`| `number`| Number of bars (length).| `context`| [`IContext`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IContext>)| PineJS execution context.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`number`

Exponential moving average of `x` with `alpha = 2 / (y + 1)`

* * *

`number`

Epsilon (machine precision). Upper bound on the relative approximation error due to rounding in floating point arithmetic.

### linreg

â¸ **linreg**(`source`, `length`, `offset`): `number`

Linear regression curve. A line that best fits the prices specified over a user-defined time period. It is calculated using the least squares method. The result of this function is calculated using the formula: `linreg = intercept + slope * (length - 1 - offset)`, where intercept and slope are the values calculated with the least squares method on source series (x argument).

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `source`| [`IPineSeries`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IPineSeries>)| Source series.| `length`| `number`| Length (number of bars back).| `offset`| `number`| Offset (number of bars)  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`number`

Linear regression curve point.

* * *

#### Returns

`number`

The natural logarithm of `x`.

#### Returns
