### rma

â¸ **rma**(`source`, `length`, `context`): `number`

Moving average used in RSI. It is the exponentially weighted moving average with `alpha = 1 / length`.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `source`| [`IPineSeries`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IPineSeries>)| Series of values to process.| `length`| `number`| Number of bars (length).| `context`| [`IContext`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IContext>)| PineJS execution context.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`number`

Exponential moving average of `x` with `alpha = 1 / y`.

* * *

### roc

â¸ **roc**(`source`, `length`): `number`

Rate of Change.

Function roc (rate of change) showing the difference between current value of `source` and the value of `source` that was `length` days ago. It is calculated by the formula: `100 * change(src, length) / src[length]`.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `source`| [`IPineSeries`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IPineSeries>)| Series of values to process.| `length`| `number`| Number of bars (length).  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`number`

The rate of change of `source` for `length` bars back.

* * *

### stoch

â¸ **stoch**(`source`, `high`, `low`, `length`, `context`): `number`

Stochastic. It is calculated by a formula: `100 * (close - lowest(low, length)) / (highest(high, length) - lowest(low, length))`

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `source`| [`IPineSeries`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IPineSeries>)| Source series.| `high`| [`IPineSeries`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IPineSeries>)| Series of high.| `low`| [`IPineSeries`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IPineSeries>)| Series of low.| `length`| `number`| Length (number of bars back).| `context`| [`IContext`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IContext>)| PineJS execution context.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`number`

Stochastic value.

* * *

### tr

â¸ **tr**(`n_handleNaN`, `ctx`): `number`

True Range

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `n_handleNaN`| `number`| How NaN values are handled. If truthy, and previous bar's close is `NaN` then tr would be calculated as current bar `high-low`. Otherwise tr would return `NaN` in such cases. Also note, that `atr` uses `tr(true)`.| `ctx`| [`IContext`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IContext>)| PineJS execution context.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`number`

True range. It is `max(high - low, abs(high - close[1]), abs(low - close[1]))`

* * *

### tsi

â¸ **tsi**(`source`, `shortLength`, `longLength`, `context`): `number`

True strength index. It uses moving averages of the underlying momentum of a financial instrument.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `source`| [`IPineSeries`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IPineSeries>)| Source series.| `shortLength`| `number`| Length (number of bars back).| `longLength`| `number`| Length (number of bars back).| `context`| [`IContext`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IContext>)| PineJS execution context.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`number`

True strength index. A value in range `[-1, 1]`

* * *

### unitId

â¸ **unitId**(`ctx`): `string`

Get the symbol unit ID.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `ctx`| [`IContext`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IContext>)| PineJS execution context.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`string`

Symbol unit ID.

* * *

### updatetime

â¸ **updatetime**(`context`): `number`

Time of the current update

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `context`| [`IContext`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IContext>)| PineJS execution context.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`number`

symbol update time

* * *

### variance

â¸ **variance**(`source`, `length`, `context`): `number`

Variance is the expectation of the squared deviation of a series from its mean `sma`, and it informally measures how far a set of numbers are spread out from their mean. Note: This is a biased estimation of sample variance.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `source`| [`IPineSeries`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IPineSeries>)| Series of values to process.| `length`| `number`| Number of bars (length).| `context`| [`IContext`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IContext>)| PineJS execution context.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`number`

Variance of `source` for `length` bars back.

* * *

### volume

â¸ **volume**(`context`): `number`

Current bar volume

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `context`| [`IContext`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IContext>)| PineJS execution context.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`number`

Current bar volume

* * *

### vwma

â¸ **vwma**(`source`, `length`, `context`): `number`

The vwma function returns volume-weighted moving average of `source` for `length` bars back. It is the same as: `sma(x * volume, y) / sma(volume, y)`

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `source`| [`IPineSeries`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IPineSeries>)| Series of values to process.| `length`| `number`| Number of bars (length).| `context`| [`IContext`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IContext>)| PineJS execution context.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`number`

Volume-weighted moving average of `source` for `length` bars back.

* * *

### weekofyear

â¸ **weekofyear**(`context`, `time?`): `number`

Week number of current bar time in exchange timezone.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `context`| [`IContext`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IContext>)| PineJS execution context.| `time?`| `number`| optional time. Current bar time will be used by default.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`number`

Week number of current bar in exchange timezone.

* * *

### wma

â¸ **wma**(`source`, `length`, `context`): `number`

The wma function returns weighted moving average of `source` for `length` bars back. In wma weighting factors decrease in arithmetical progression.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `source`| [`IPineSeries`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IPineSeries>)| Series of values to process.| `length`| `number`| Number of bars (length).| `context`| [`IContext`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IContext>)| PineJS execution context.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`number`

Weighted moving average of `series` for `length` bars back.

* * *

### year

â¸ **year**(`context`, `time?`): `number`

Year of current bar time in exchange timezone.

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `context`| [`IContext`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IContext>)| PineJS execution context.| `time?`| `number`| optional time. Current bar time will be used by default.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`number`

Current bar year in exchange timezone.

* * *

### zigzag

â¸ **zigzag**(`n_deviation`, `n_depth`, `context`): `number`

Zig-zag pivot points

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `n_deviation`| `number`| Deviation| `n_depth`| `number`| Depth (integer)| `context`| [`IContext`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IContext>)| PineJS execution context.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`number`

the zig-zag pivot points

* * *

### zigzagbars

â¸ **zigzagbars**(`n_deviation`, `n_depth`, `context`): `number`

Zig-zag pivot points

#### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `n_deviation`| `number`| Deviation| `n_depth`| `number`| Depth (integer)| `context`| [`IContext`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IContext>)| PineJS execution context.  
---|---|---  
  
#### Returns

`number`

the zig-zag pivot points (for bars)

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.PivotPointsStandardIndicatorOverrides

# Interface: PivotPointsStandardIndicatorOverrides

[Charting Library](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library>).PivotPointsStandardIndicatorOverrides

Overrides for the 'Pivot Points Standard' indicator.

Use these properties to customize indicator via [IChartWidgetApi.createStudy](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#createstudy>) and [IStudyApi.applyOverrides](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IStudyApi#applyoverrides>).

## Indexable

âª [key: `string`]: [`StudyOverrideValueType`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#studyoverridevaluetype>)


---

[Broker](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker>).Position

Describes a single position.

## Hierarchy

  * [`PositionBase`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PositionBase>)

  * [`CustomFields`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.CustomFields>)

â³ **`Position`**


## Properties

### avgPrice

â¢ **avgPrice** : `number`

The weighted average price of all positions for a symbol. The library uses this value to draw a position line on the chart and to calculate [profit and loss values](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/profit-and-loss>).

#### Inherited from

[PositionBase](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PositionBase>).[avgPrice](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PositionBase#avgprice>)

[Exit levels](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/brackets#multiple-exit-levels>) associated with the position. Used when [BrokerConfigFlags.supportMultipleExitLevels](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportmultipleexitlevels>) is enabled.

#### Inherited from

[PositionBase](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PositionBase>).[exitLevels](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PositionBase#exitlevels>)

* * *

### id

â¢ **id** : `string`

Position ID. Usually id should be equal to brokerSymbol

#### Inherited from

[PositionBase](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PositionBase>).[id](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PositionBase#id>)

* * *

### longQty

â¢ `Optional` **longQty** : `number`

Long position quantity

#### Inherited from

[PositionBase](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PositionBase>).[longQty](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PositionBase#longqty>)

* * *

### message

â¢ `Optional` **message** : [`OrderOrPositionMessage`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.OrderOrPositionMessage>)

Message describing the state of the position

#### Inherited from

[PositionBase](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PositionBase>).[message](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PositionBase#message>)

* * *

### qty

â¢ **qty** : `number`

Position Quantity (positive number)

#### Inherited from

[PositionBase](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PositionBase>).[qty](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PositionBase#qty>)

* * *

### shortQty

Short position quantity

#### Inherited from

[PositionBase](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PositionBase>).[shortQty](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PositionBase#shortqty>)

* * *

### side

â¢ **side** : [`Side`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Broker.Side>)

Position Side

#### Inherited from

[PositionBase](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PositionBase>).[side](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PositionBase#side>)

* * *

### symbol

â¢ **symbol** : `string`

Symbol name

[PositionBase](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PositionBase>).[symbol](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PositionBase#symbol>)

* * *

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PositionDialogOptions

# Interface: PositionDialogOptions

[Broker](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker>).PositionDialogOptions

## Hierarchy

  * [`TradingDialogOptions`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.TradingDialogOptions>)

â³ **`PositionDialogOptions`**


## Properties

### customFields

â¢ `Optional` **customFields** : [`TradingDialogCustomField`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker#tradingdialogcustomfield>)[]

Custom fields that are displayed in the Order Ticket. Refer to the [Add custom fields](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/order-ticket#add-custom-fields>) section for more information.

**Example**
    
    
    customFields: [  
        {  
            inputType: 'TextWithCheckBox',  
            id: '2410',  
            title: 'Digital Signature',  
            placeHolder: 'Enter your personal digital signature',  
            value: {  
                text: '',  
                checked: false,  
            },  
            customInfo: {  
                asterix: true,  
                checkboxTitle: 'Save',  
            },  
        }  
    ]  
    

#### Inherited from

[TradingDialogOptions](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.TradingDialogOptions>).[customFields](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.TradingDialogOptions#customfields>)

* * *

### maxExitLevelsCount

â¢ `Optional` **maxExitLevelsCount** : `number`

Specifies the maximum number of [exit levels](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/brackets#multiple-exit-levels>) (brackets) that can be attached to a single parent order or position.

#### Inherited from

[TradingDialogOptions](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.TradingDialogOptions>).[maxExitLevelsCount](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.TradingDialogOptions#maxexitlevelscount>)


---

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.PositionPercents

# Interface: PositionPercents

[Charting Library](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library>).PositionPercents

Anchored (fixed) drawing point position as a percentage from the top left of a chart. For example `{ x: 0.5, y: 0.5 }` for the centre of the chart.

## Properties

### x

â¢ **x** : `number`

Position as a percentage from the left edge of the chart.

* * *

### y

â¢ **y** : `number`

Position as a percentage from the top edge of the chart.

### format

â¢ `Readonly` **format** : [`StudyPlotValueFormat`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#studyplotvalueformat>)

A type of data that an indicator displays, such as `volume` or `price`. Values on the [Price Scale](</charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/Price-Scale>) depend on this data type. Additionally, you can adjust a precision of indicator values. To do this, specify the `precision` property in [StudyPlotValuePrecisionFormat](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyPlotValuePrecisionFormat>).

For more information about `format`, refer to the [Metainfo](</charting-library-docs/latest/custom_studies/metainfo/>) article.

### ohlcPlots

â¢ `Optional` `Readonly` **ohlcPlots** : [`MappedObject`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.MappedObject>)<`Readonly`<[`StudyOhlcStylesInfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyOhlcStylesInfo>)>>

array with study plots info. See dedicated article: [Custom Studies OHLC Plots](</charting-library-docs/latest/custom_studies/Custom-Studies-OHLC-Plots>)

#### Inherited from

[RawStudyMetaInfoBase](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase>).[ohlcPlots](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase#ohlcplots>)

* * *

### palettes

â¢ `Optional` `Readonly` **palettes** : [`MappedObject`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.MappedObject>)<`Readonly`<[`StudyPalettesInfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyPalettesInfo>)>>

definitions of palettes that are used in plots and defaults. Palettes allows you use different styles (not only colors) for each line point.

This object contains palette names as keys, and palette info as values: `[palette.name]: { colors, valToIndex, addDefaultColor }`, where

  * `colors`* - an object `{ [color_id]: { name: 'name' }}`, where name is a string that will appear on Style tab of study properties dialog.
  * `valToIndex` \- an object, the mapping between the values that are returned by the script and palette colors.
  * `addDefaultColor` \- boolean, if true the defaults are used for colorer type plot, when its value is null or undefined.


#### Inherited from

[RawStudyMetaInfoBase](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase>).[palettes](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase#palettes>)

* * *

### plots

â¢ `Optional` `Readonly` **plots** : readonly `Readonly`<[`StudyPlotInformation`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#studyplotinformation>)>[]

array with study plots info. See dedicated article: [Custom Studies Plots](</charting-library-docs/latest/custom_studies/Custom-Studies-Plots>)

#### Inherited from

[RawStudyMetaInfoBase](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase>).[plots](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase#plots>)

* * *

### precision

â¢ `Optional` `Readonly` **precision** : `string` | `number`

Use [format](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfo#format>) instead.

**`Deprecated`**

#### Inherited from

* * *

### priceScale

â¢ `Optional` `Readonly` **priceScale** : [`StudyTargetPriceScale`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.StudyTargetPriceScale>)

Price scale to use for the study

#### Inherited from

[RawStudyMetaInfoBase](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase>).[priceScale](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase#pricescale>)

* * *

### shortDescription

â¢ `Readonly` **shortDescription** : `string`

Short description of the study. Will be displayed on the chart

#### Inherited from

[RawStudyMetaInfoBase](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase>).[shortDescription](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase#shortdescription>)

* * *

### styles

â¢ `Optional` `Readonly` **styles** : [`MappedObject`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.MappedObject>)<`Readonly`<[`StudyStylesInfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStylesInfo>)>>

* * *

### symbolSource

â¢ `Optional` `Readonly` **symbolSource** : [`SymbolInputSymbolSource`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolInputSymbolSource>)

Symbol source

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RegressionTrendIndicatorOverrides

# Interface: RegressionTrendIndicatorOverrides

[Charting Library](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library>).RegressionTrendIndicatorOverrides

Overrides for the 'Regression Trend' indicator.

Use these properties to customize indicator via [IChartWidgetApi.createStudy](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#createstudy>) and [IStudyApi.applyOverrides](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IStudyApi#applyoverrides>).

## Indexable

âª [key: `string`]: [`StudyOverrideValueType`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#studyoverridevaluetype>)


---

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SMIErgodicIndicatorOscillatorIndicatorOverrides

# Interface: SMIErgodicIndicatorOscillatorIndicatorOverrides

[Charting Library](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library>).SMIErgodicIndicatorOscillatorIndicatorOverrides

Overrides for the 'SMI Ergodic Indicator/Oscillator' indicator.

Use these properties to customize indicator via [IChartWidgetApi.createStudy](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#createstudy>) and [IStudyApi.applyOverrides](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IStudyApi#applyoverrides>).

Default value: `15`

* * *

### indicator.linestyle

â¢ **indicator.linestyle** : `number`

Default value: `0`

* * *

### indicator.linewidth

â¢ **indicator.linewidth** : `number`

Default value: `1`

* * *

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SaveLineToolsContext

# Interface: SaveLineToolsContext

[Charting Library](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library>).SaveLineToolsContext

Additional information for the [IExternalSaveLoadAdapter.saveLineToolsAndGroups](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IExternalSaveLoadAdapter#savelinetoolsandgroups>) request.

## Properties

### requestId

â¢ **requestId** : `string`

A unique identifier for the save request that can be used for debugging and logging purposes.

* * *

### sharingMode

â¢ **sharingMode** : [`SharingMode`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.SharingMode>)

The sharing mode for the current save request. The library uses this property to determine how to handle the saved data, and your adapter uses it to route the data to the correct storage destination.

* * *

### symbol

â¢ `Optional` **symbol** : `string`

The name or identifier of the symbol displayed as the main series on the chart. Use this value to determine where or how to save the data.

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SearchSymbolResultItem

# Interface: SearchSymbolResultItem

[Charting Library](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library>).SearchSymbolResultItem

[Symbol Search](</charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/Symbol-Search>) result item. Pass the resulting array to the callback for [`searchSymbols`](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/datafeed-api/required-methods#searchsymbols>) or [`searchSymbolsPaginated`](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/datafeed-api/additional-methods#searchsymbolspaginated>).

**`Example`**
    
    
    {  
    	description: 'Apple Inc.',  
    	exchange: 'NasdaqNM',  
    	symbol: 'AAPL',  
    	ticker: 'AAPL',  
    	type: 'stock',  
    }  
    

## Properties

### description

â¢ **description** : `string`

Description

* * *

### exchange

â¢ **exchange** : `string`

Exchange name

* * *

### exchange_logo

â¢ `Optional` **exchange_logo** : `string`

URL of image to be displayed as the logo for the exchange. The `show_exchange_logos` featureset needs to be enabled for this to be visible in the UI.

The image should ideally be square in dimension. You can use any image type which the browser supports natively. Simple SVG images are recommended.

Examples:

  * `https://yourserver.com/exchangeLogo.svg`
  * `/images/myImage.png`
  * `data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3...`
  * `data:image/jpeg;base64,/9j/4AAQSkZJRgABAQAASABIAAD/4...`


* * *

### logo_urls

â¢ `Optional` **logo_urls** : [`string`, `string`] | [`string`]

URL of image/s to be displayed as the logo/s for the symbol. The `show_symbol_logos` featureset needs to be enabled for this to be visible in the UI.

  * If a single url is returned then that url will solely be used to display the symbol logo.
  * If two urls are provided then the images will be displayed as two partially overlapping circles with the first url appearing on top. This is typically used for FOREX where you would like to display two country flags as the symbol logo.


The image/s should ideally be square in dimension. You can use any image type which the browser supports natively. Simple SVG images are recommended.

Examples:

  * `https://yourserver.com/symbolName.svg`
  * `/images/myImage.png`
  * `data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3...`
  * `data:image/jpeg;base64,/9j/4AAQSkZJRgABAQAASABIAAD/4...`


* * *

### symbol

â¢ **symbol** : `string`

Short symbol name

* * *

### ticker

â¢ `Optional` **ticker** : `string`

It is a unique identifier for a particular symbol in your [symbology](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/Symbology>).

You should avoid using colons (":") in ticker values unless you are following the TradingView format: "NYSE:IBM". Using colons may cause unexpected behaviour and display bugs.

Corresponds with [LibrarySymbolInfo.ticker](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#ticker>).

* * *

### type

â¢ **type** : `string`

Type of symbol

'stock' | 'futures' | 'forex' | 'index'


---

    }  
    

## Properties

Description

Exchange name

### exchange_logo

URL of image to be displayed as the logo for the exchange. The `show_exchange_logos` featureset needs to be enabled for this to be visible in the UI.

The image should ideally be square in dimension. You can use any image type which the browser supports natively. Simple SVG images are recommended.

  * `data:image/jpeg;base64,/9j/4AAQSkZJRgABAQAASABIAAD/4...`

URL of image/s to be displayed as the logo/s for the symbol. The `show_symbol_logos` featureset needs to be enabled for this to be visible in the UI.

  * If a single url is returned then that url will solely be used to display the symbol logo.
  * If two urls are provided then the images will be displayed as two partially overlapping circles with the first url appearing on top. This is typically used for FOREX where you would like to display two country flags as the symbol logo.


The image/s should ideally be square in dimension. You can use any image type which the browser supports natively. Simple SVG images are recommended.

Examples:

  * `/images/myImage.png`

  * `data:image/jpeg;base64,/9j/4AAQSkZJRgABAQAASABIAAD/4...`


* * *

### symbol

â¢ **symbol** : `string`

* * *

### ticker

â¢ `Optional` **ticker** : `string`

It is a unique identifier for a particular symbol in your [symbology](</charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/Symbology>).

You should avoid using colons (":") in ticker values unless you are following the TradingView format: "NYSE:IBM". Using colons may cause unexpected behaviour and display bugs.

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SetVisibleRangeOptions

# Interface: SetVisibleRangeOptions

[Charting Library](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library>).SetVisibleRangeOptions

Options for setting the visible range.

Setting `applyDefaultRightMargin` or `percentRightMargin` will result in the `to` value of the range specified being ignored and the timestamp of the latest bar on the chart being used instead.

## Properties

### applyDefaultRightMargin

â¢ `Optional` **applyDefaultRightMargin** : `boolean`

Apply the default right offset (margin) when setting the range.

* * *

### percentRightMargin

â¢ `Optional` **percentRightMargin** : `number`

Apply a percentage right offset (margin) when setting the range.

* * *

### rejectByTimeout

â¢ `Optional` **rejectByTimeout** : `number`

In the current implementation, we cannot capture all errors during the `SetVisibleRange` process, which means we cannot guarantee that the process will always be resolved or rejected. To address this issue, you can use the `rejectByTimeout` option to ensure rejection after a specified timeout.


---

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.SingleBrokerMetaInfo

# Interface: SingleBrokerMetaInfo

[Broker](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker>).SingleBrokerMetaInfo

## Properties

### configFlags

â¢ **configFlags** : [`BrokerConfigFlags`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags>)

Broker Configuration Flags

* * *

### customNotificationFields

â¢ `Optional` **customNotificationFields** : `string`[]

Optional field. You can use it if you have custom fields in orders or positions that should be taken into account when showing notifications.

**`Example`**

if you have field `additionalType` in orders and you want the chart to show a notification when it is changed, you should set:
    
    
    customNotificationFields: ['additionalType']  
    

* * *

### customUI

â¢ `Optional` **customUI** : [`BrokerCustomUI`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerCustomUI>)

This optional field can be used to replace the standard Order Ticket and the Add Protection dialogs with your own. Values of the following two fields are functions that are called by the Trading Platform to show the dialogs. Each function shows a dialog and returns a `Promise` object that should be resolved when the operation is finished or cancelled.

**NOTE:** The returned `Promise` object should be resolved with either `true` or `false` value.

**`Example`**
    
    
    customUI: {  
        showOrderDialog?: (order: Order, focus?: OrderTicketFocusControl) => Promise<boolean>;  
        showPositionDialog?: (position: Position | IndividualPosition, brackets: Brackets, focus?: OrderTicketFocusControl) => Promise<boolean>;  
        showCancelOrderDialog?: (order: Order) => Promise<boolean>;  
        showClosePositionDialog?: (position: Position | IndividualPosition) => Promise<boolean>;  
    }  
    

* * *

### durations

â¢ `Optional` **durations** : [`OrderDurationMetaInfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.OrderDurationMetaInfo>)[]

List of order duration options that determine how long the order remains active. Specifying `durations` enables a drop-down menu in the Order Ticket for supported orders. Refer to [Enable Time in force menu](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/order-ticket#enable-time-in-force-menu>) for more information.

The objects have the following keys: `{ name, value, hasDatePicker?, hasTimePicker?, default?, supportedOrderTypes? }`.

* * *

### orderRules

â¢ `Optional` **orderRules** : [`OrderRule`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.OrderRule>)[]

Order Rules

* * *

### tradedGroupConfig

â¢ `Optional` **tradedGroupConfig** : [`TradedGroupConfig`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.TradedGroupConfig>)

A traded group is a combination of elements that are drawn on the chart for single position or order.

It represents a position or order along with its brackets, plus all the UI controlsâsuch as TP/SL buttons, various lines, and dotsâthat indicate where the position's brackets are located and what is currently selected.


---

### customUI

â¢ `Optional` **customUI** : [`BrokerCustomUI`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerCustomUI>)

This optional field can be used to replace the standard Order Ticket and the Add Protection dialogs with your own. Values of the following two fields are functions that are called by the Trading Platform to show the dialogs. Each function shows a dialog and returns a `Promise` object that should be resolved when the operation is finished or cancelled.

**NOTE:** The returned `Promise` object should be resolved with either `true` or `false` value.

**`Example`**
    
    
    customUI: {  
        showOrderDialog?: (order: Order, focus?: OrderTicketFocusControl) => Promise<boolean>;  
        showPositionDialog?: (position: Position | IndividualPosition, brackets: Brackets, focus?: OrderTicketFocusControl) => Promise<boolean>;  
        showCancelOrderDialog?: (order: Order) => Promise<boolean>;  
        showClosePositionDialog?: (position: Position | IndividualPosition) => Promise<boolean>;  
    }  
    

* * *

### durations

â¢ `Optional` **durations** : [`OrderDurationMetaInfo`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.OrderDurationMetaInfo>)[]

List of order duration options that determine how long the order remains active. Specifying `durations` enables a drop-down menu in the Order Ticket for supported orders. Refer to [Enable Time in force menu](</charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/order-ticket#enable-time-in-force-menu>) for more information.

The objects have the following keys: `{ name, value, hasDatePicker?, hasTimePicker?, default?, supportedOrderTypes? }`.

* * *

### orderRules

â¢ `Optional` **orderRules** : [`OrderRule`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.OrderRule>)[]

Order Rules

* * *

### tradedGroupConfig

â¢ `Optional` **tradedGroupConfig** : [`TradedGroupConfig`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TradedGroupConfig>)

A traded group is a combination of elements that are drawn on the chart for single position or order.

It represents a position or order along with its brackets, plus all the UI controlsâsuch as TP/SL buttons, various lines, and dotsâthat indicate where the position's brackets are located and what is currently selected.


---

## Properties

### active

â¢ `Optional` `Readonly` **active** : `boolean`

Whether the input should be rendered based on user inputs

* * *

### confirm

â¢ `Optional` `Readonly` **confirm** : `boolean`

if true, then user will be asked to confirm input value before indicator is added to chart. Default value is false.

* * *

### defval

â¢ `Optional` `Readonly` **defval** : [`StudyInputValue`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#studyinputvalue>)

default value of the input variable. It has the specific type for a given input and can be optional.

* * *

### group

â¢ `Optional` `Readonly` **group** : `string`

Creates a header above all inputs that share the same `group` string. This string is used as the header's display text in the _Settings_ dialog.

* * *

### hideWhenPlotsHidden

â¢ `Optional` `Readonly` **hideWhenPlotsHidden** : `string`[]

An array of plot ids, upon the hiding of which, this input should also be hidden within the legend

* * *

### id

â¢ `Readonly` **id** : `string`

Id for the input

* * *

### isAlwaysShownInLegend

â¢ `Optional` `Readonly` **isAlwaysShownInLegend** : `boolean`

Is the input always visible in the legend, even when the user hides indicator inputs in the legend

* * *

### isHidden

â¢ `Optional` `Readonly` **isHidden** : `boolean`

Is the input hidden

* * *

### name

â¢ `Readonly` **name** : `string`

Title of the input

* * *

### type

â¢ `Readonly` **type** : [`StudyInputType`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.StudyInputType>)

Input type

* * *

### visible

â¢ `Optional` `Readonly` **visible** : `string`

Is the input visible

  * Default value: `5`
  * Input type: `float`
  * Min: `0.001`
  * Max: `100`


* * *

### zig zag.plot.color

â¢ **zig zag.plot.color** : `string`

Default value: `#2196F3`

* * *

### zig zag.plot.display

â¢ **zig zag.plot.display** : `number`

Default value: `15`

* * *

### zig zag.plot.linestyle

â¢ **zig zag.plot.linestyle** : `number`

Default value: `0`

* * *

### zig zag.plot.linewidth

â¢ **zig zag.plot.linewidth** : `number`

Default value: `2`

* * *

### zig zag.plot.plottype

â¢ **zig zag.plot.plottype** : [`LineStudyPlotStyleName`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#linestudyplotstylename>)

Default value: `line`

* * *

### zig zag.plot.trackprice

â¢ **zig zag.plot.trackprice** : `boolean`

Default value: `false`

* * *

### zig zag.plot.transparency

â¢ **zig zag.plot.transparency** : `number`

Default value: `0`


---

# Source: https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStylesInfo

# Interface: StudyStylesInfo

[Charting Library](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library>).StudyStylesInfo

Study styles description.

## Properties

### char

â¢ `Optional` `Readonly` **char** : `string`

Char to display with the plot. Applicable only to chars plot types.

* * *

### forceOverlay

â¢ `Optional` `Readonly` **forceOverlay** : `boolean`

optional flag to indicate the plot should be drawn on main page

* * *

### format

â¢ `Optional` `Readonly` **format** : `Partial`<[`StudyPlotValuePrecisionFormat`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyPlotValuePrecisionFormat>)>

Info about the Price Scale formatting

* * *

### histogramBase

â¢ `Optional` `Readonly` **histogramBase** : `number`

Histogram base. A price value which will be considered as a start base point when rendering plot with histogram, columns or area style.

* * *

### isHidden

â¢ `Optional` `Readonly` **isHidden** : `boolean`

If `true` then the styles tab will be hidden in the study dialog.

* * *

### joinPoints

â¢ `Optional` `Readonly` **joinPoints** : `boolean`

If `true` then plot points will be joined with line, applicable only to `Circles` and `Cross` type plots. Default is false.

* * *

### maxHeight

â¢ `Optional` `Readonly` **maxHeight** : `number`

Maximum possible plot arrow size. Applicable only to arrow plot types.

* * *

### minHeight

â¢ `Optional` `Readonly` **minHeight** : `number`

Minimum possible plot arrow size. Applicable only to arrow plot types.

* * *

### showLast

â¢ `Optional` `Readonly` **showLast** : `number`

If defined, defines the number of bars to plot on chart.

* * *

### size

â¢ `Optional` `Readonly` **size** : `number` | [`PlotSymbolSize`](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.PlotSymbolSize>)

Size of characters on the chart. Possible values are: `auto`, `tiny`, `small`, `normal`, `large`,`huge`. Applicable to `chars` and `shapes` plot types.

* * *

### text

â¢ `Optional` `Readonly` **text** : `string`

Text to display with the plot. Applicable to `chars` and `shapes` plot types.

* * *

### title

â¢ `Readonly` **title** : `string`

Title used in the study dialog styles tab.

* * *

### zorder

â¢ `Optional` `Readonly` **zorder** : `number`

Used to control the zorder of the plot. Control if a plot is visually behind or in front of another.


---

### chart_theme_changed

â¢ **chart_theme_changed** : (`themeName`: `string`, `isStandardTheme`: `boolean`, `onlyActiveChart`: `boolean`) => `void`

#### Type declaration

â¸ (`themeName`, `isStandardTheme`, `onlyActiveChart`): `void`

Fired when the chart theme is updated. This event can be triggered in several ways:

  * When a user applies a saved [chart template](</charting-library-docs/latest/saving_loading/#chart-templates>).
  * When the chart theme is reset to the default settings. (`isStandardTheme` parameter will be true)
  * When the [IChartingLibraryWidget.changeTheme](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget#changetheme>) method is called.
  * When the [IChartWidgetApi.loadChartTemplate](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#loadcharttemplate>) method is called.


##### Parameters

Name| Type| Description| `themeName`| `string`| The name of the chart theme or template that was applied.| `isStandardTheme`| `boolean`| `true` if the default built-in theme was restored; `false`, if a custom chart template was applied.| `onlyActiveChart`| `boolean`| `false`, when applied to all charts  
---|---|---  
  
##### Returns

`void`

* * *

Overrides for the 'SuperTrend' indicator.

Use these properties to customize indicator via [IChartWidgetApi.createStudy](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#createstudy>) and [IStudyApi.applyOverrides](</charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IStudyApi#applyoverrides>).
